It is alright. You have to try a lot harder than usual to make friends compared to other colleges. It is quite independent.

It’s cheap tuition and the only school like it for the price. With good widely respected programs especially if you plan on staying in nyc/bk for your career. However what you save in tuition you pay for with administrative and management nightmares. The first year or the pre requisite year is a nightmare. They accept way too many students and just assume the majority will fall off by the time it’s ready to apply to actual programs. Nursing, dentist and radiology tech are so competitive they don’t have to care about most students

The campus actually feels like a college campus despite being in the middle of Manhattan, with access to green spaces and old architecture. So far my teachers have all been very kind, understanding and insightful (despite a bit of disorganization, but it is just the beginning of the semester). It's kind of hard to navigate around campus or at least the NAC which is where all my classes are, but I'm getting the hang of it. Looking forward to exploring more of the resources offered like clubs, study abroad, food pantry, and making new connections. I've heard there are strong ties with various institutions and organizations, including Columbia University. Despite being a very hilly area I've been enjoying commuting by bike, and there are ample places to safely park it during class. It's kind of close to many trains which is really convenient. One thing I wish could be improved is how crowded it gets during events like orientation and welcome back day (crowd control and time management)
CUNY City College of New York is a peaceful environment that is conductive to learning. There are many knowledgeable and kind professors who are willing to help you. The campus is also beautiful.

I have thoroughly enjoyed my experience so far at CCNY. There are lots of great professors who have made the classes more engaging and enriching for me and many other students as well. One thing I would to change is the lack of reaching out from the college. Most of the time I feel like I am doing everything blind. If it was more organized and the school helped students get acclimated to their new environment, I feel that the experience would be smoother and less puzzling.

Best school for future scientists and engineers. This school is likely the most academically rigorous institution but it is worth it. No where else with the density in talent, opportunity, and training to truly think and work like a nobel laureate.

Unfortunately, I haven't had the opportunity to attend yet, but the onboarding process has been rewarding. I have been assigned a degree program and given the steps to complete the application process.

Cal-Tech is a cool place with great alumni who have had great impact in the world. Their student body are also friendly to international students that makes us feel a little bit relieved and we feel at home.

I did things and experienced things that had previously not been within my subjective world of possibilities. It is a very supportive place, well-endowed with resources, not least of which is the dedicated, highly competent, bleeding edge community. For someone whose interest in science, technology, and the frontiers of human cognition verges on obsession, it exists in the foremost tier of locations and institutions.

Caltech is amazing. It often gets put into the box of being for weird nerds, but in general people are really normal. There’s a lot of fun events and outgoing people. It’s certainly challenging academically but having most assignments be collaborative it isn’t too stressful. Caltech is definitely a unique college experience, but everyone will have a different college experience wherever they go so that’s no a huge factor. The professors are great and most really care about their students. Overall, Caltech is a great school I would highly recommend.
Seem to be very accommodating to different students needs which I found to be very good and helpful.
I like the campus most , students activities , diverse programs of alumni and dorms and libraries . overall experiences are very good .
It will be a lovely experience for me to study there and also enjoy the beautiful state and learn more about the society

I was in the Class of 1996. I had such a difficult time due to personal issues. I wish someone had been able to explain to me that it is okay to take a break. That would have been better than failing out and then taking a break. Also, peer support at the counseling center would have made a difference.
It’s amazing amazing school full of people who are at the top of their field and passionate about sharing their knowledge with the students.
Great school, amazing opportunities provided by all the faculty. Beautiful campus perfect for anyone around :)
The stereotype of ‘Caltech is lot of work and a lot of theory’ is not exaggerated. Good if you really enjoy doing math and science, but genuinely do not go here if you value your free time.
It's a challenging school, but one with lots of opportunities to be taken advantage of, and a research student's dream come to life.
Great academic and research environment. Amazing people all around, from students to faculty. Only problem is the administration.
Incredible place. The brightest minds working together to advance scientific progress all in a beautiful, warm neighborhood.

ai_summary: California State University - East Bay has made strides in improving student life , transitioning from its roots as a commuter school with enhanced student housing and campus events. Academically, many reviews highlight supportive professors who genuinely care about student success and smaller class sizes that facilitate learning. However, some students mention challenges with disorganization, particularly regarding enrollment and class availability. The campus is noted for its diversity and community atmosphere, which many find enriching, though it may lack the vibrant social scene typical of larger, non-commuter colleges. Student services receive mixed feedback; while most find faculty helpful and friendly, some experiences indicate a need for better communication and coordination. Tuition is generally considered affordable, making CSUEB an appealing choice for many, especially those looking for a balance between academics and a supportive environment.

reviews.text: This university is generally a commuter school and it definitely reflects that culture. Most people that go here are from the local area so if you are looking for a good local school in the East Bay, CSU East Bay is a great choice. However if you are looking for a typical college experience this university may not be for you. There isn't a lot that goes on on campus in terms of community engagement and clubs. Internships and other opportunities are a little harder to find and you really have to do the work yourself to get those opportunities. Professors are happy to talk and help out but if you aren't use to putting in work to find yourself opportunities, you may struggle to get the most out of your education here. This university is truly what you make of it so if you want community and loads of opportunities you will have to foster that environment yourself. Classes are great here, professors are awesome, and even administrative staff are super helpful.

I loved the psychology department faculty. They were very supportive, open, and available in assisting the students in whatever manner possible. I was a student with chronic health problems and though some of my professors where not privy to exactly what I was battling, they noticed I had issues and were always reaching out to me so that I did not fall too behind. There isn’t much I would change with my experience at CSU East Bay. I wished they offered the graduate degree I am pursuing.

I was accepted the same week classes started and taking online classes, everything so far has been quite positive. The support this campus has for their students is impressive and I feel welcomed and supported overall. I am so exciited for my first college "experience!"

I am a transfer student and the process went smoothly for me. I enjoyed the orientation process and I liked how they offered to help student search for their classes prior to starting the semester. The campus is big and pretty quiet which I like.

My experience with CSU East Bay has been excellent. They are the only college that has a Spanish program online. I am getting my degree online because I have physical disabilities that prevent me from going in person. The online platform for classes is great and my professors have all been awesome.

I liked how accessible professors were for help with assignments, having a smaller class size was so important for me. Everyone was always so willing to help. Get your student ID (physical and the online one). Definitely join a club or two. One related to your major and one that sounds interesting to you. There are many events that are held by different clubs, I would say definitely try and attend a few events. It is so worth it, the atmosphere is so amazing. When you get your counselor for your major, make sure you have all your classes planned out and stay on top of your assignments. The tutors who are available for help are great, along with the other resources for assignment help. For something I would like to see change, I would say please bring back the shuttle service. It was very nice to have, especially for night classes.
I would love to see the facility get updated, the school chairs and tables are outdated, and the classrooms tend to be on the smaller size.

Pursuing my online bachelor’s at CSU East Bay has been a genuinely positive reset for me. The flexibility lets me keep working while staying consistent with my classes, and the structure pushes me to manage my time like an adult instead of relying on last-minute talent. Being able to log in after work, stay on pace with weekly deadlines, and see my grades climb has made school feel achievable and worth the effort.

My experience at Cal State East Bay has been a mix of strong creative growth and a lot of self-discovery. The faculty in the art and design programs genuinely care about students, and many classes push you to explore new mediums like 2D, 3D, 4D, and digital, which really helped me build a diverse portfolio. I’ve appreciated how supportive and approachable my professors are, especially when it comes to project feedback and professional development.
What I’d like to see improve is consistency in course scheduling and clearer communication around program requirements. Sometimes it’s difficult to balance everything when key classes overlap or aren’t offered as often. More institutional support for art students, like access to updated equipment, software, and workspace hours would make a big difference. Overall, though, CSUEB has helped me grow as both an artist and a student.
I like it i guess, i live near so it is just a walk away which is pretty easy. other than that not much on strong feelings although my professors are still getting used to new tech

As an art student, I learned that the school likes to cut funding to the arts department before any of the other departments on campus, leading many of the students floundering at the lack of available classes they need to graduate. I was told to focus on my major's classes before going into the general education portion specifically because of this, however that would have caused severe senior-itus, and there is a high probability I would have dropped out if I had listened to that counselor. Get your GE requirements out of the way as fast as you can so that the rest of your time on campus is focused entirely on your degree.
Aside from being forced to take classes in one of the oldest buildings on campus, teeming with mold and asbestos, the art building holds a lot of really amazing professors, ready and willing to help their students succeed in any way they can.

For my short time so far at East Bay for my 1st year I can’t complain. The teachers are not rude and inconsiderate nor overwhelm you and with thousands of homework chapters after. This campus is a “chill school”. There’s not a whole lot of bigger parties within the school, but that’s good if you’re not into that life and just to focus on studies. Walking in I didn’t know how many people I would see who would look or act like me, but this school has a decent amount of diversity. It just depends what time of the day.

CSU East Bay offers a diverse and welcoming campus environment with supportive professors and strong opportunities for hands-on learning, internships, and career preparation. Students appreciate the Bay Area location and scenic campus views. However, as a largely commuter school, campus life can feel less lively compared to more residential universities. Some students also mention challenges with housing, administrative processes, and staying connected if they commute or take online classes. Overall, CSUEB is seen as a practical, inclusive, and career-focused university experience.
I was a full student but I just to went to my classes the days and time that I had to go. I didn't have a lot of experience in the campus life.
East Bay has a beautiful campus with amazing views of the bay. As a commuter student, I dont get to see much of the campus life.

ai_summary: California State University - Fresno has a vibrant and diverse community that many students appreciate. The campus is seen as welcoming, with plenty of resources available to support academic and personal development, such as counseling services, tutoring, and student organizations. While students often mention strong school spirit and various extracurricular activities, some express concerns about the quality of professors and the accessibility of certain courses due to high enrollment. Additionally, while the campus offers a beautiful environment and convenient size, several reviews point out issues with parking and food options, indicating room for improvement in student services. Overall, students feel a sense of belonging and support at Fresno State, making it a favorable option for many.

reviews.text: Overall, my experience at Fresno State was positive. The professors within the History department were all phenomenal and helped my peers and me a tremendous amount. They always went above and beyond to give us the best quality education they could. I did not go to too many school events, so I cannot speak on campus life as a whole, but within my own section, making friends was easy. Great School.

California State University, Fresno is an amazing choice to complete a four year degree program or even a masters degree. There are a variety of program options available for students to choose from and a great campus for students to immerse themselves in a learning environment. However, if you’re planning on enrolling a program that requires high levels of physics and calculus. You are sure to complain of the lack of teaching many of the physics professors provide. Additionally, if either field is not your strong suit, you will struggle indefinitely. Regardless, once through with those courses, you’ll enjoy the experiences you had and will be grateful you attended this university.

My first semester was great. I loved all my classes. They were definitely a challenge. However, the teachers made the classes interesting and the course work felt assessable. The school seems over crowded as classes were difficult to get. However, the school did what it could to guarantee me full time student status at 16 units.

I encountered a professor that made me feel not welcomed in her class. As a first generation student from my family I have some questions and don't know who I can reach out to for help. I am trying to navigate this experience with the help of advisor.

My time at Fresno State was a meaningful chapter of my life; supportive, inspiring, and deeply personal. Professors were not only experts but compassionate mentors who took time to understand each student. When I lost my Tio one year, their empathy and flexibility helped me grieve while staying on track academically. Campus life was equally wonderful: convenient food options like Dunkin’, Starbucks, Subway (my go‑to), Panda, and many others made mornings easy. Fresno State’s vibrant diversity showed up in campus art and cultural events, creating a welcoming community rich with different perspectives. The care from faculty, everyday comforts, and lively, diverse campus culture made my college experience unforgettable.

I think that Fresno State is a vibrant campus that many future college students would love to experience. There is a place for everyone, whether you like video game theory, marching band, theatre, or have any other interests. That being said, I do think that there needs to be a better way to research these kinds of groups of clubs on the official Fresno State website, because sometimes people will never hear of them if they have nothing to do with their major or field of study. With that fixed, people will be able to find their own little community that they never would have been able to be supported by otherwise.

At Fresno state I really like the community. Their is almost always events going on around campus for you to get involved with and make new friends at. Additionally the. Staff/ peer mentor here are truly invested in seeing you succeed. They have plenty of resources to help you do so, and are ready to lend a war if that's all you need. Overall it's an very supportive community here at Fresno state.

Being at California State University, Fresno has been a really positive experience for me. Coming here as an international student, I was a little nervous at first, but the professors, staff, and my classmates made me feel welcome and supported. I’ve learned so much, especially in my pre-health courses, and the resources on campus—like tutoring and advising—have really helped me grow. Beyond academics, I’ve enjoyed being part of a diverse community and getting involved in campus life. Overall, Fresno State has not only strengthened my knowledge but also made me feel like I truly belong here while motivating me to keep working toward my goals in the health field.

My experience at Fresno State was truly meaningful. What I loved most was the strong sense of community. There is something for everyone, and it is easy to make friends and feel connected on campus. Whether it is through clubs, classes, or student events, there are always opportunities to find where you belong. I built friendships that still mean a lot to me, and being surrounded by people who support and encourage you makes a big difference.
If I could change one thing, I would want the academics to challenge students a little more and push them to think deeper. However, I still appreciate how much I learned and how supported I felt. Fresno State is more than a school; it is a community that teaches you about resilience, leadership, and connection. Now, as an alumna who works on campus, I see how much Fresno State continues to grow, and I am proud to still be part of that change.

Fresno State is a great place with lots of opportunities to move further with your education. Professor and staff help you navigate to the right path to achieving your goals. Campus is very nice, clean and peaceful. Serval helpful and fun events are held to help the students.
My overall experience has been great. I was pretty worried of not getting along with my roommates but we are like best friend. The hall food is good too and my class aren't to bad either.
One thing i can say about California State University of Fresno is that it offers a wide variety of options to help me succeed in my business career.
I think it is a good course; almost all classes are conducted on campus, and for the most part, getting involved is really fun and easy.

California State University- Fresno is a very calm and peaceful environment. There are many places where students can relax or socialize, as well as areas that offer quiet spaces for studying or working. There are also some very good food options in and next to the university.

I had a great experience at Fresno state. It was affordable and close to home. This is where I met a lot of my close friends and that I will continue to be friends with. I would recommend Fresno state to anyone who lives in the Central Valley.

ai_summary: California State University, Fullerton offers a vibrant and diverse campus experience , with many students appreciating the supportive faculty and the resources available for academic and personal development. Reviewers commonly highlight the approachable professors and the quality of education, though opinions vary on class sizes and the engagement level in general education courses. The student life is enriched by various clubs and activities, but many note that it feels like a commuter school , leading to challenges in forming social connections unless actively involved in campus events. Parking emerges as a significant concern , as it can be difficult, especially during peak hours. The campus environment is generally praised for its cleanliness and pleasant atmosphere, with many appreciating the proximity to local amenities and recreational opportunities. While tuition is deemed affordable compared to other schools, some students express frustration with class availability and the functioning of financial aid services. Overall, CSUF presents a solid option for students seeking a diverse atmosphere, dedicated faculty, and a variety of academic programs, albeit with some logistical challenges.

reviews.text: I love the diversity of the students and faculty. I like the inclusivity and the amount of student resources the school offers. I like the availability of parking options. I do wish that they were a bit better at keeping the classrooms cleaner.

My experience with CSUF has been nothing short of life changing. I went from a shy girl from a small town to an outgoing individual with lots of friends. I am a double majoring honors student, and the honors program has been so supported and helpful towards my goals and aspirations!

At California State University, Fullerton, I have had great experiences with meeting new people and professors! I have made so many new friends, and everyone on campus is super friendly. I have not had a bad professor! All of my professors have been super kind and welcoming to students. All of the clubs seem super interesting, and the one I am a part of, Full-Out, has been such an amazing experience. However, I think campus events could be better advertised. Everything is through an Instagram/TikTok post, and if you do not follow those accounts, you will never know the events are happening.

Living on campus at Cal State Fullerton has completely shaped my college experience. As a first year student living in residence halls, I've built friendships that feel like family and have found a strong sense of community. Being able to walk to class within minutes is extremely convenient and has allowed me to focus on academics and involvement instead of parking or traffic. Although CSUF is not a traditional "college town," it offers incredible opportunities. The campus is beautiful, with so many food/study spots, and the weather is always nice. Located in Orange County, we're close to beaches, diverse cuisines, and so many activities. Most importantly, my professors have been supportive, passionate, and invested in my growth, making CSUF a place where I truly thrive.
I liked how everyone was very welcoming. The professors are very helpful when you communicate with them. The only thing I didn’t like was my gap, I had a four hour gap and it was horrible.

My overall experience was complicated. There were good parts and bad parts. Take any review with a grain of salt. Despite being a commuter school, though, I did meet some of the best people in my life.

During my time at Cal State Fullerton, I was able to experience new prompts to projects and learn about new materials and processes at the same time. The professors pushed for developing your skills while at the same time developing your overall artistic abilities. The studios are always refreshed during the semester breaks and always in immaculate condition before starting a new semester.
There are a variety of resources that one can benefit from. The university is diverse making anyone feel included.

It has been great so far all the professor want to help you succeed and the school provided in your tuition with free tutoring either in person or online. The first week of school is a little hectic because people on the waiting list are in that class hoping people don’t show up and it could get a little cramped. The student live is actually pretty active every Tuesday they have a good market with a lots of good options and every week there are people in stands resale.

I have only been at this university for a semester, but coming from a community College, it was a big adjustment. People are welcoming and kind in the classroom, but there is no social life outside of the classroom. If you don’t live there, which half of us don’t, it’s really hard to make friends.

CSUF is a great school! The professors I had cared about their students education and were supportive. I liked the campus, though some of the older buildings, the lower/basement levels especially, do need renovation. The Arboretum is a fantastic and unique addition to the campus as well to study, relax, and explore.
Amazing campus! Not only a great school academically, but has so many extracurriculars, whether it be intermural sports, clubs, activities, etc. Truly an amazing school overall!

Great budget school for the local socal community. Many different events the school provide students for job outreach and internships. Needs more archaeology professors with the anthropology department.

My overall experience at California State University, Fullerton (CSUF) has been largely positive and rewarding. CSUF offers a diverse and welcoming campus community, and I appreciated how inclusive and supportive the environment felt. Many professors were knowledgeable, approachable, and genuinely invested in student success, especially those with real-world industry experience who brought practical insights into the classroom. The campus resources—such as the library, career center, and student organizations—were helpful and contributed to both academic and personal growth.

As a Business Major at CSUF, the number of opportunities and support to transition to your desired career after school is more than abundant. Taking advantage of these opportunities by attending and networking at events will make your time at CSUF worthwhile. Yes, the workload for some classes can be very heavy and overwhelming at times throughout the semester, but it is also manageable.

ai_summary: California State University - Long Beach offers a vibrant campus atmosphere marked by friendly and welcoming students and staff. Many reviews highlight the beautiful campus location near the beach, as well as a strong emphasis on diversity and community involvement through clubs and events. Academic experiences can be mixed; while some students rave about their professors and the variety of courses offered, others express concerns about the quality of instruction in specific departments and the inconsistent guidance from academic advisors. The university is viewed as a solid choice for both its affordability and the opportunities available , such as internships and extracurricular activities, although some students mention challenges with parking and navigating the commuter-focused environment. Overall, CSULB is characterized as a supportive place where students can develop personally and academically.

reviews.text: I graduated with a BFA in Drawing and Painting from the School of Art at Cal State Long Beach. I was a transfer student, meaning that I transferred after completing my first 2 years at a community college. The transition into CSULB was smooth, although I transferred during the pandemic. The 1st of the 3-year program was completed online due to quarantine restrictions, but despite the limitations, my teachers made it a pleasant experience. Once the pandemic restrictions were lifted, we transferred our studies to on-site, and I had the best experience. I received everything I was looking for and more. I highly recommend CSULB to anyone interested in achieving similar career goals.
I really enjoyed my time at Cal State Long Beach. I got involved right away in clubs and dorming on campus and that helped me make a lot of connections.

CSULB engineering program is by far the best in education and career preparation. I studied Electrical Engineering and was able to immerse myself in the course work that gave a well rounded engineering experience.

The overall experience has been good, but lately there have been a lot of changes that have ruined the school. We're paying a higher tuition for construction that some of us won't even be able to access because we will have graduated. They've also removed a lot of the restaurants and we're left with overpriced food trucks.

Overall, I really like the campus; it's very unique and moderately sized. I also really like the professors; most professors are well-equipped, and they care about their students. The only thing I would change is the closure of most dining or food places around campus due to the construction. Additionally, I don't understand why students still have to pay the $500 every semester for the student union building if it's closed down. We currently don't have access to the buiding yet we are required to pay for it.

As a commuting student, I was not involved as much on campus. The school itself is mainly a commuter school as well, with many going to school and either going to work or back home if they live nearby. During my time there, I find it difficult to consistently maintain friendships since people come and go or are busy. There are clubs on campus, but it does feel a bit clique-y a bit. As in life, you have to put effort and energy into cultivating college relationships with your peers and professors. As for food options, there are a few fast casual and campus-specific restaurants. Being a commuter school, parking is always a problem, especially if you come to school after 10 AM. Parking structures, lots, and even street parking are going to be full, and you will have to spend time driving around to find parking as if you were at a sold-out concert. The campus is very beautiful, and being near the beach provides great weather and scenery. The area around campus feels very safe.
CSU Long Beach is a nurturing environment for a diversity of students. The campus is generally a safe area to experience college life.

My overall experience with california state university long beach was great. I went on these past three years on avid field trips. I really liked the campus, student life, and classes. Once I arrived at Cal State Long Beach for the first time, I fell in love with the campus and its locals. I really liked Long Beach because I heard that the university had good programs for the nursing major, which is something I would like to pursue, as I am passionate about helping others. There are many people who are currently struggling and can't afford a necessary check-up. Going to California state university long beach made me want to accomplish my own dreams of being a nurse and becoming a first gen college student in the family, because I would want my mother to be proud of me.
I enjoy going here. The campus is beautiful and there is so much to do and many fun clubs to join on campus. Everyone is very welcoming.

Overall, my experience at CSULB is amazing. I’ve met many wonderful friends and had many memories around and off campus. However, speaking truthfully the surrounding area can be somewhat dangerous and even dirty in some areas.

Lots of resources (tutoring, academic advising, food, etc.), professors genuinely want to see you succeed as long as you are putting effort into your studies and your overall success. The campus is large, but there are student shuttles that run through campus. Overall great experience.

My overall experience was strong. Professors were well-educated and supported me throughout my academic endeavors. My classmates were kind and open to building connections for study groups, project development, and overall support in my well-being. The campus itself was very welcoming, and the diversity and culture was very inclusive. Campus events were inclusive, accommodating to all needs of each unique individual. I particularly enjoyed the Not Alone @ the Beach club and the SRWC.
Honestly, it’s been not bad. Although yes, there’s been lots of construction, I’ve still been able to walk around and find places to eat or chat with my friends.

At CSULB, I have taken a lot of great courses taught by great professors. Most of the students I have interacted with are also very kind and open. My only complaint is that the campus life is a rather boring.
I had a good experience attending Cal State Long Beach. I was an Art major student. I am grateful for the teachers and staff that supported me through my 4 years of school.

ai_summary: California State University, San Bernardino, is noted for its supportive and welcoming community , with many students highlighting the university's commitment to helping them succeed academically. The professors are generally seen as caring and engaged, fostering a positive learning environment . Despite a lack of vibrant campus life and social events, particularly for those outside of Greek life, there are numerous clubs and activities available to get involved. The campus enjoys a clean and safe atmosphere , with helpful staff and resources catering to students' needs. However, some concerns were raised about limited diversity , issues with counseling services, and crowded online classes, plus the affordability of food options and tuition hikes. Overall, CSUSB offers a unique blend of strong academics and a sense of community, although it may not be the ideal choice for students seeking a bustling social scene.

reviews.text:
My time at California State University, San Bernardino was both peaceful and productive. At first, I focused mainly on attending classes, doing my work, and seeking help from professors when needed. Over time, I discovered that CSUSB offers many opportunities beyond the classroom. The university provides lab and research opportunities with professors who genuinely want students to succeed. Even after graduating, I was able to continue working with faculty and graduate students to strengthen my CV and prepare for graduate school.
I also appreciated the comfortable and welcoming campus environment. The newer buildings and study spaces made the campus feel modern and peaceful, which helped make going to school enjoyable.
One improvement would be making campus opportunities more visible to students. As a commuter student, it was easy to overlook posters and events. More consistent communication through student email could help students stay informed about the many resources CSUSB offers.

Considering my second semester in this campus, living on dorms, it has been quite solid. Usually cleanliness levels are there, open spaces with many free resources, and honestly just great place to hang sometimes.
Studying at CSUSB have gave me an insight of balance with work and school. All my professor were very nice, reached out to me when I was struggling and helped me with my academics.

I love how my campus has many resources where they allow us to get free groceries or snacks, give us financial advice, resources to help guide students how to get food stamps cash aid, etc. what I would say is that I wish they would announce these resources because many students on this campus don’t know about them which is something I feel would be very resourceful for us as students. I say this because many students who come to CSUSB are minorities that are in need of more assistance and I love how the school offers these resources but many students don’t know about them and they don’t get taken advantage of. Not only that but I simply love the environment of my campus especially when we have events such us career day where the campus provides information on how to apply to jobs, how to create a resume, and if there are any jobs available on campus. I love how our campus is a community where we strive to uplift each other which i find crucial especially during times like these.

I enjoyed the atmosphere and the architecture of CSUSB's campus; it was wonderful. I would definitely address parking, as it was not very accessible the first month of school. I would also hope to see more food option changes in the future for their prospective students. It was a very open campus, but it was a part of a good community surrounding it.
Online classes were made easy. Professors did a good job at organising the course and I knew the expectations from the start.

I really enjoyed just being at a university and grateful I was accepted. The area it is located in is not the safest, campus food spots closed early, and they are doing major construction by the freeway so getting there was a hassle. I was in the Sociology deprtment and I can count on how many professors were helpful. Academic advising was no help, I practically guided myself with what classes I needed to take. I am first gen going to college so it was hard finding the resources.
When I went to the university campus I was very surprised by the size of it. I liked the buildings they had and their cafeteria.
Many individuals may talk bad about the school because it is in San Bernardino, but it truly is a great school.

Hello readers actually I really enjoy attending Csusb. The staff is caring not like traditional
Boring schools, they offer help to students and have so many resources to help you along the way. I enjoy CSUSB, and its community

I enjoy attending CSUSB because of the diversity and the fact that the professors are willing to help in anything you need help with. They are there for you and there is lot of resources to help you out.

My experience at CSUSB has motivated me to strive for excellence and continuous growth. As a double major in Anthropology, a Dean’s List student, a participant in the Sociology Honors class, and a proud member of ALPACA, I have developed a deep appreciation for learning, research, and community engagement. The professors at CSUSB have been truly inspiring, and each one has contributed to my academic and personal development by encouraging me to think critically and approach challenges with curiosity and determination.
Through my studies and campus involvement, I have gained valuable knowledge and hands-on experience that have strengthened my passion for understanding people, cultures, and the world around me. CSUSB has provided an environment where I have been able to grow both academically and personally. I am grateful for the mentorship, support, and opportunities I have received, which continue to motivate me to pursue success and contribute positively to my community and field.

I am a transfer student, and even though I have only been here at California State University, San Bernardino (CSUSB) for almost a year and a half, my experience has been great. What I liked most about CSUSB is the various opportunities to get involved in research. Throughout my experience here, I have had the opportunity to work with excellent professors in their research labs. I have enjoyed seeing how invested the professors are in helping students attain their academic goals.
I would like to see improvement in the academic advising. My experience with advising at CSUSB was not the best, as I did not have an academic advisor for most of my time there. I also found that advisors did not have answers to many of my questions and were uncertain about how to help me. It would be beneficial for students at CSUSB to receive better academic advising to succeed at CSUSB.

Most of the aspects in Cal State San Bernardino are great! Most of the professors are good at teaching and seem to love their job, the people attending CSUSB are kind, and the education I have been receiving has been valuable. There are some things that I would like to see changed, the main one being the price of parking. Students have to pay $399 for a full year parking permit and professors have disclosed with me that they pay the same price. I believe this price is completely unfair and students have voiced that they need the price down, yet nothing has been done. I would like to see where the money from all the parking permits goes, since there are areas of the campus that are dirty and broken and have yet to be fixed. That Is my biggest concern regarding this campus.

So far, everything has been well. As a student who is pursuing their Single Subject Teaching Credential, I am very thankful for the professors in the program because they are very dedicated to us - the students. It's not often that I see professors who advocate for their students as the professors in my program do.

ai_summary: Campbell University offers a unique college experience characterized by a strong sense of community and supportive faculty . Many students appreciate the small class sizes, which allow for more personalized interactions with professors who are invested in their students' success. The campus itself is described as beautiful and safe, fostering a family-like atmosphere among students. While the academics receive high praise, particularly for programs in health sciences and education, there are common concerns about the cost of attendance and limited food options around campus. Student life is vibrant, with opportunities for involvement in various clubs and activities, although some express a desire for more entertainment options nearby. Overall, Campbell University is viewed as a great place for those seeking a tight-knit community and dedicated academic support .

reviews.text: Campbell University is a university that does best at specialized professional academic programs including, Engineering, Trust and Wealth Management, PGM, and an array of medical programs . These programs are what CU is known for and will continue to pursue. The environment that was marketed in admissions does not accurately account for the Christian integration in CU's student life. There are many opportunities to get involved and to work hard, to build a social life, and begin professional networking. There is not a huge presence of Greek life, party scenes, or the popular college experience.

When I arrived at Campbell University I felt like I was at home. They constantly checked in on me and made me feel like I was apart of a family there. The professors were incredibly kind and loved on me as a student. They saw me as a person and not just someone in the classroom. The cummunity on campus is seen through all the activities and student life oppertunities that Campbell has to offer. Campbell University is place I can call my home away from home as the place feels like one giant family.

Visiting Campbell University was one of the most inspiring and transformative experiences of my life, providing me with a clear vision of my academic, personal, and professional goals. From the moment I stepped onto the campus, I was struck by the sense of community, the dedication of the faculty, and the energy of the students. Walking through the classrooms, residence halls, and campus facilities, I immediately felt a welcoming environment that encouraged learning, collaboration, and personal growth. It was clear that Campbell University is not just an institution for education but a community that fosters purpose, service, and leadership.
One of the most memorable aspects of my visit was the opportunity to interact with professors who are passionate about their fields and genuinely invested in their students’ success.

If you’re looking for a close community where everyone smiles at you when you walk past and professors know your name I would suggest Campbell. So far, the adjustment process has been easy because there are so many resources and events to help you feel more comfortable in your first weeks. Since being at Campbell I have joined clubs like Student Government, Poetry, and club Lacrosse. Campbell offers so much and presents it exclusively. There are many options for meal exchanges if you do not want to eat in the cafe everyday there is Starbucks, sushi, chick fila, Moes, the spread, and irresistabowls. All included in your meal plan. Friendships are easy to find since it is such a close community here, but I will say Campbell is definitely not a party school. We do not throw during weekdays and sometimes weekends are iffy. Other than that Campbell is a great school I recommend for furthering your career and self.

Campbell is a small college in the middle of no where. It takes about 14 minutes to get to the closest Walmart and 20 till you get to any different food selections if wanted. Despite how far things are, Campbell is a quiet college where you can honestly get lost into work because there’s nothing to do. If im not mixed in with football then doing some type of work. Theres little to no parties and there’s almost no school spirit unless it’s a decent sports match or good cause. If anything were to change I would love to see more activity around campus. Let People see the fun that can be had a Campbell so they can feel at home.

Campbell University is a small private school in Buies Creek, NC. I enjoyed the small, close-knit community and local events. The classes are small, therefore, it is easier for students to build personal relationships with their professors. My main concern is the lack of food options, such as restaurants, fast food, and grocery stores. Besides that, I had a great overall experience.

Campbell University's online programs offer a flexible and supportive educational experience tailored for adult learners, working professionals, and military-affiliated students. Courses are delivered asynchronously through the Blackboard platform, allowing students to engage with coursework on their own schedules while meeting assignment deadlines. Each student begins with the Campbell University Online Seminar for Undergraduates, an orientation designed to familiarize them with the online learning environment and available resources .
Students benefit from 24/7 online tutoring in core subjects via Pear Deck Tutor (formerly TutorMe), ensuring academic support is always accessible. The university's Office of Student Success provides comprehensive services, including academic advising, career services, and disability accommodations, fostering a supportive community that prioritizes student achievement .
Overall, Campbell University is the place to be.
I am an athlete at Campbell, and I love how much support we get as athletes. Students benefit from individualized attention and strong relationships with professors.

The food sucks only when we have visitors they prepare good food in Gaylords kitchen, the kitchen staff treat you terribly, professors and staff do not treat all races the same and you can’t do nothing about it.
White dominant school
Lack of creative programs
Campus safety constantly ticketing students
Financial office staff are the rudest
Very expensive and value doesn’t match the price
Location is also in a country town away from everything and lack of food resources.

My overall experience at Campbell University was to be put bluntly, amazing and not something I would trade for anything. I had so many amazing experiences through CAB and my classes. In which I have met so many amazing and thoughtful people, that I would consider, friends for life. These people are truly wonderful, kind, and caring individuals. They have made me a better person and further confirmed the career that I want to go into. The memories that I have made are some of the best that I have ever made and ones that I will never forget.

If you are a transfer student with more than a handful of credits I would highly dissuade you from trying to finish a bachelors here. The community of non traditional students is great but housing is sparse and expensive and the limited availability of classes (read: not offered frequently enough, not enough professors) means that you will likely take much longer to finish your degree than at a larger school. Additionally, summer school is practically impossible to swing unless you have endless cash.

Campbell University is overall, a wonderful school. This University has a gorgeous campus that holds a ton of events such as laser tag, a formal, movie nights, food trucks, and so much more. Campbell holds a lot of different clubs for different ethnic groups, religious groups, and even ones for different hobbies on the side which allows us students to meet a lot of new people and create a circle full of lifelong friends. My experience with academics here have been a little rocky. It definitely is a lot different from any other classes I have ever taken but just know that you will feel very accomplished. Some of the professors are hard to follow and may move at a faster pace than you can handle but office hours and different tutoring opportunities are given to students to help us succeed and stay on track. I would like to see more growth in the activities on and off campus because there isn't always a lot to do. I would also like for classes to be a tad bit lighter and cheaper.

I have enjoyed my time at Campbell so far. The professors and friends you make along the way care so deeply for you and will do everything they can to see you do your best in every aspect of your life. I am very thankful that I chose to complete my undergrad here at Campbell. Through my time here, I have grown so much as a person, and I would not change my time here if I could!

Campbell University is a wonderful place to be, i have alot of mind blowing experiences at the university.
WHAT I LIKED
Their students are very neat and coordinated,i will like all of my children to attend champbell University.
WHAT NEEDS TO BE IMPROVED
I think they should improve on the security of the students.
My experience at Campbell has been a humbling experience to get to meet new people and also learn more things about the campus and the community as a whole

Capitol Technology University is definitely more of an academic school than a party school, which was helpful for me. Despite being a commuter, I was still heavily involved in campus life. The professors are typically adjunct professors who work in industry, which is ideal for getting connected to hiring companies.

Capitol Tech is for a very niche type of student. No, there's not a party scene; no, there's not a sports team. Capitol Tech is the school you go to to learn about your discipline and get employed after. If you want the traditional college experience with parties and sporting events, that's not Capitol. Capitol is the school where you get to live in a nice apartment with like-minded people living for the most part independently.

I'm happy with how things are taught at the school. The lecturers are fantastic, and I constantly look forward to their lessons. The campus radiates a sense of community among students. This makes us feel like we belong, and that means a lot to me.
Pretty good but I wish the financial aid was better for graduate students. They need more grants and not loans.

My first year at this university was incredibly enjoyable, especially for those who are intellectually curious. Thanks to the wide range of opportunities available in various fields. I highly recommend this university to everyone.
It's a great university. It is intellectually tough, but the students also know how to relax and have fun on weekends. It was an excellent place to study, and the atmosphere was fantastic!
I had the best experience at Capitol Technology University! Seriously, I've never felt happier. This school is ideal for anyone serious about their studies while also wanting to have a good time.

One thing I appreciate about Capitol Tech is the availability of faculty for assistance. They are always willing to go above and beyond to help students understand difficult concepts and provide additional resources for further learning. This level of dedication truly sets this university apart from others.

As an independent learner, I appreciate the flexibility of their online courses. The virtual classroom environment is well-organized and provides a seamless learning experience. The instructors are also highly responsive, making it easy to communicate and stay on track with coursework.

I’m constantly amazed by the stunning campus at Capitol Technology University. It's not just about aesthetics, but also the serene environment that fosters a conducive learning atmosphere. Whether you're studying in the library or taking a walk around campus, there's always a sense of inspiration and motivation to excel here.

I liked that the classrooms were small so that it would be easier to learn lessons. From what I have seen so far, I believe that the institute is completely geared towards helping students reach their academic goals.

Capitol Tech's curriculum is designed to integrate practical skills with theoretical knowledge, providing students with a well-rounded education that prepares them for real-world applications. As someone who has experienced this firsthand, I highly recommend this university to individuals seeking an immersive learning experience.

The best things about it-
● Financial aid and scholarships are amazing
● Their commitment to military students is outstanding.
● It offers a perfect blend of technical education and liberal arts.

As a current PhD student at Capitol Technology University, I can confidently say that the faculty here are experts in their fields and have been instrumental in my academic journey. Their guidance and support have been invaluable as I navigate through my dissertation. This is definitely the place to be if you're looking for top-notch professors who truly care about your success.

Capitol technology university is an all technology based major which allows each student to focus and hone their skills in whatever field they aspire to go into, with hands on experience in each labs.

ai_summary: Carnegie Mellon University is often described as a rigorous academic institution where students with diverse interests —from computer science to fine arts—can thrive. The academic workload is challenging, but many appreciate the quality of education and the opportunity to learn from knowledgeable professors who are dedicated to student success. The campus environment is generally tight-knit, fostering a sense of community, although the social scene can be lacking, with some students noting a limited party culture and a focus on academics over socializing. While there are numerous clubs and extracurricular activities, some reviews suggest that the cost of attendance and the pressure to succeed can create a high-stress atmosphere . Despite this, students value the networking opportunities and supportive faculty, and many feel that the intense experience ultimately prepares them well for their future careers. Overall, CMU seems to appeal to those who are truly passionate about their chosen fields and who are ready for the accompanying challenges.

reviews.text: I think that attending Carnegie Mellon was a wonderful choice. I was in the biology department, and found the professors - especially those who taught lab courses - to be great teachers who had great in-depth levels of knowledge regarding the experiments. Some of them have been by greatest mentors and guides years after I've graduated.
Carnegie Mellon has very rigorous academics and overall good professors who, in my experience, are understanding and helpful. The social scene, however, is pretty underwhelming

I am a freshman at Carnegie Mellon University. CMU offers a rigorous academic environment with nice facilities and strong resources. Its moderate size fosters close connections, while the diverse student body enriches campus culture. Student life is vibrant and collaborative, though dorms could benefit from modernization and upgrades to better match the university’s overall excellence and innovation.

There is no institutional support for student success or to help you navigate and design a course of study. I can’t imagine a worse experience and lower quality of education. My major had a bunch of requirements that were all nonsense taught by adjuncts and no one at the school helped us figure out how to take courses with actual content. Graduate school here was treated like a joke. Now a few years out, I can say without a doubt that the entire experience was a waste of time and significantly derailed my career. Its horrible to acknowledge that and to imagine what could have been different if I had gone to a real school.

In our Chinese parent community, multiple cross-admits to MIT and Stanford ultimately chose Carnegie Mellon’s School of Computer Science (SCS) — including my son. After two full years at CMU, I can say this with confidence: it’s been the best decision for our family.
Yes, it’s rigorous — by design.
My son came in with a strong background (USACO finalist, USAMO, IOI), and still found upper-level algorithms and AI courses humbling in the best way. That healthy stretch is exactly what we wanted: depth that forces real growth, not just easy A’s.
Bottom line: If your student thrives on real systems, wants early research with world-class labs, and values an outcomes-driven culture with strong startup support, CMU SCS delivers.

Carnegie Mellon University has a strong music program, with many talented faculty from the Pittsburgh Symphony. The rigor of the School of Music is good preparation for a career in music, but the administration is not always on top of organization and communication.

Biggest mistake you can make in your life. Don’t be tricked by the school’s marketing. Most of the programs are not supported or developed well. No real university culture and lots of departments are basically just for show. Reputation is only in CS and robotics and any other interest area is largely considered a joke. The school has no regard for students as humans and just views them as $$$.
Carnegie Mellon, as is well known, had a very challenging academic education. Those challeneges really prepare you to face anything post college and setbyou up for success.

My experience at Carnegie Mellon University has been very rewarding. The professors are knowledgeable and supportive, and the hybrid course structure has been a lifesaver for me as a mother managing family responsibilities without a support system. Being able to choose between in-person and online sessions allowed me to stay engaged without compromising my family duties. Group projects and collaboration were well-managed, even remotely. I would love to see more flexible timing options and enhanced online interaction for hybrid students. Overall, CMU provides a rigorous yet adaptable learning experience for those balancing demanding personal commitments.

I liked some of my professors as well as the percussion studio. Everyone in the studio is very accepting of me and my disability. The percussion professors have caused me to make a lot of progress this year with my playing. I would like to see the disability accommodations at CMU change. Not getting the housing and classroom accommodations that I need caused me to be hospitalized at the end of my first semester. I faced a lot of discrimination from one of my professors (who was the only professor for a four semester required class). People with disabilities already have a very rough time going through school. That shouldn't be made worse by accommodations being denied. I am not the only one affected by this. I have a friend, who after having multiple surgeries this year and a heart condition, can not get a parking spot in the lot by our academic building.

The school is in a really nice area of Pittsburgh that has a fair amount of fun places to explore. Campus itself is pretty nice overall; although, the cold/rainy weather during the fall and winter months can put a real damper on the social scene, the campus becomes really lively during the springtime when the weather warms up. There are lots of great opportunities in STEM and research that are provided, so If you're passionate about STEM--especially computer science or engineering--then this is the perfect school for you.

Carnegie Mellon has been a very welcoming and amazing community. The teachers all want you to succeed, but are willing to push you hard. I found friends very easily and feel so connected to everyone I've met, even just for a short time.
Whatever you do, don’t talk to David Chickering and John Hannon. They will interfere in your academic career and it could cause irreparable damage to your future and life.
Academically rigorous place but you can find people like you. Felt like the counselors really gave me the help I needed to succeed.

The faculty at CMU School of Art went out of their way to destroy my career, time and time again. As an alum, faculty interfered in my job search, spread malicious information to future employers and collaborators, and directly intervened to prevent me from getting a PhD at MIT. I would have never imagined that a university would devote so many resources to damaging their own alumni, but in this case, attending CMU led to some of the most horrifying experiences of my life.

ai_summary: Reviews of Carson-Newman University highlight a strong sense of community , with many students expressing that the close-knit environment fosters meaningful relationships with both peers and professors. Academically, the smaller class sizes provide a personal touch , allowing for one-on-one interactions and a supportive learning atmosphere where faculty are invested in student success. However, some reviews mention that challenges remain, including concerns about the quality of dormitories, limited food options, and high tuition costs, which have impacted some students' decisions to continue their studies. The campus is appreciated for its beauty and the variety of activities available, but there are also mentions of outdated facilities and a lack of diversity. Overall, Carson-Newman is regarded as a supportive, Christ-centered institution suitable for those who value a personal education and community engagement.

reviews.text: It was a great experience. Especially the nursing program. The professors were academically accomplished. They shared easier ways to remember things.

The environment at Carson-Newman is like family. The faculty really care and are super helpful. It's a great environment to learn, and I recommend Carson-Newman to anyone looking for a small liberal art school.
I love Carson Newman! The classes are small so the professors actually know your name and care about your grades

A great faith-based school overall. Professors push students to their full potential but also show grace to students and are largely very approachable. Small class sizes are very beneficial to overall learning. Carson-Newman has students from a large variety of backgrounds and creates a welcoming environment for everyone. There are a large variety of events on campus that appeal to different interests, from those that connect cultures across the world to professional music performances to athletic events to Bible-centered events and chapel services. The food is really good for a college campus, and though there is some room for improvement there is always something for even pickier eaters.

For my time on campus I enjoy it very much. I typically only spend time on campus for classes and work that I cant work on at home. I do enjoy homecoming and go to that it is fun to see everyone of all ages come together and celebrate this school. It is usually nice and beautiful on them days. I have liked my professors thus far and hope I don't come across any I don't like. They have been nothing but helpful to me when I have needed help. I enjoy the scenery and campus and plan to fin ish out my degree at this institution.

The professors are really caring and are very supportive. The school environment is very welcoming and supportive as well. I feel safe every time I am there. They also have security on the campus which make me feels safe especially because they have an app for your phone that can pinpoint your location.

The campus is beautiful and I love how small the community is. The professors are so helpful and we have amazing resources such as free tutoring, therapy, and clinics. The only main complaint Is how they handle situations when they arise. We are kept in the dark and find things out when the news does. The older dorms are also covered in mold. The new dorms are much nicer, but were clearly built cheap as they have only been lived in a couple months and pain is peeling and the floor tiling is raising up. However, I would say that the good outweighs the bad.
It has allowed me to grow in the field of interest and develop the skills I need to become an administrator.

The teachers and the stuff is well respectful and kind and welcoming
When you need help they will help no matter what it is
I've enjoyed my experience attending Carson-Newman University and have no complaints! The professors have been especially helpful for my studies.

I live really close to campus, and am doing dual enrollment there. I am also going to start working there in fall semester. When I toured the campus it was a really fun experience. My family also works on campus, and they thoroughly enjoy the environment, work, and pay.

Carson Newman is a small connected university. The dorm living is being upgraded but not available yet. I would like to see more food options besides Cafe. The professors and staff seem very interested in you and willing to help.

I enjoy attending Carson-Newman University as I like being in an institution that holds up biblical values. Most of the professors are nice, and as a music major, I am proud to be connected with the school's great music department and one of the greatest college choirs in the state of Tennessee. If I were to change some things, I would like for the internet to be better and for the buildings on campus to be updated sooner.
The administrative staff, volleyball coaches, volleyball players, and admissions representatives have all been outstanding, friendly, and very helpful.
When you get to Carson-Newman they tell you that it is going to be more than just a school it is going to turn into a family and a home and they are one hundred percent correct while saying that.

ai_summary: Case Western Reserve University offers a strong academic experience , particularly for STEM majors, with engaged professors who are dedicated to student success. Reviewers appreciate the hands-on clinical opportunities available, especially in nursing and engineering, and the abundance of research and internship connections thanks to nearby healthcare institutions and businesses. However, there are consistent criticisms regarding the food quality, aging dormitories, and a perceived lack of school spirit and social life. The environment is described as academically rigorous and competitive, attracting high-achieving students. While the campus is accessible and located in a vibrant area with cultural attractions, some reviews express concerns about safety and diversity among the student body, alongside a desire for more inclusive and supportive social activities . Overall, Case Western combines academic rigor with a supportive community, though some students wish for improvements in non-academic aspects like dining and student engagement.

reviews.text: Geek culture is huge here. But pepeole also party and have fun. If you're smart and geeky, you would love it here. Salaries out of college are very high. I think every type of student will at least like CWRU.

The campus itself is beautiful and located in University Circle, which gives students access to museums, hospitals, and cultural institutions. Cleveland isn’t a traditional college town, but it has plenty to offer if you explore, including sports, food, and arts scenes. Campus safety is taken seriously, though some surrounding areas require awareness.
Social life exists, but it’s not the center of campus culture. Many students find their community through clubs, research labs, Greek life, or shared academic interests rather than big parties. Overall, Case Western is an excellent fit for students who prioritize academics, research, and career preparation over a traditional partyschool experience.

It is a good university. They have modern facilities and great research. World famous for medical and engineering. There is so much diversity within the students, there are a lot of asians in particular.

My entire experience has been great (professors classmates prestige etc) So I’ll only mention the few things I’d like to see improved
I wish they would make Luetner dining hall better. Our other dining hall, fribley, is really good, but people who live on northside need to walk all the way across campus to go there.
I also want them to make it so that doors don’t need a key card during peak hours. They just changed this this semester, and it has made getting into some buildings hard.
That’s all the issues I have with the college I have, I really love cwru otherwise. I just hope the admin fixes these things.
Party scene is a bit lacking. Besides that it’s been an amazing experience. Students are very ambitious and smart. Professors are too.

We don’t have school spirit in the traditional sense. Instead of big football games, we have hackathons and such. The two biggest events are springfest (a party in the spring) and a 24 hour sci fi movie marathon. I love it here, it would be a much more famous place if it was in cali or boston

My professor gave me this to fill out. That being said YOU SHOULD APPLY! I never thought I could get in to a prestigious engineering school, but I applied and got in! It’s so much about luck.
Stanford is a better college than us, and my friend got into stanford and rejected from cwru. It really is just random. They reject people for no reason.
I am a biomedical engineering major. Academics are hard, but so far I have a 4.0. It has been extremely challenging to get this though.
Social life: amazing! I really feel like I belong. A lot of people here are “cool nerds”
Cleveland is a cool city. Some neighborhoods are dangerous, so don’t go wandering around the city by yourself. But you really should explore some of the safe areas if you come, they’re amazing! Cwru is in a very safe area.
In conclusion, you should apply at least! Even if you don’t think you can get in, it’s always worth a shot. And there’s no essays.

Wonderful people (super collaborative environment), super kind professors. Extremely underrated dance, theatre, and music programs. Extremely well rated engineering and medical programs. NOT JUST STEM SCHOOL!! 33% are humanities/social sciences! TONS of resources and excellent housing options, especially as you go up in years. Free counseling, week long orientation which is beautiful and wonderful. winters are cold though lol_
All CWRU students have their own unique passions and interests. A lot of diversity, of every kind. It's a great university for anyone who wants to learn arts, sciences, or engineering.
Case is a good college. A lot of students here are biomedical engineers. STEM is good all around, business and law are good too

Cwru is in Cleveland Ohio. It’s not in downtown Cleveland, it’s in a neighborhood called university circle. Cleveland is an interesting city, though a lot of it is unsafe. Campus is completely safe though.
The academics are good. It seems more oriented to people going to graduate school.
I’ve been pleasantly surprised by my experience. The community is really welcoming. The weather is cold, but the people are warm.

There is less presence of alcohol than there is at other universities. There is still drinking that goes on, and partying too. But it does not define the campus life. I once heard a local say even if a CWRU student is drunk they are still smarter than you. I don't drink, but I have really liked my time here, there are plenty of other things to do

My daughter is a sophomore at Case and could not be happier. She has had fantastic classes and has made so many wonderful connections with faculty members in her majors (Economics and Political Science) as well as receiving great guidance from her study abroad advisor. The academics are challenging (they should be), but she is doing exceptionally well and enjoying her experience. She is involved in numerous campus activities (Greek Life, although it's not the type of drunken Greek Life experience that most people imagine), tour guiding, and more. Cleveland has a lot to offer and she has attended lots of events on and off campus. The food has been surprisingly good, with lots of variety and additional options all around campus. Case has lots of resources, but is also close-knit enough for students to feel connected and "at home". Case is not cheap, but it has been worth every penny thus far. Housing isn't the most luxurious, but it's good enough. A+

A top university for anything relating to health. Nursing, Premed, Bio/Neruobio research, it is very famous and well known. I can not speak to other areas (I am in premed) but I have had an excellent experience here. Classes are hard, but graduates compete w grads from CMU, UChicago, and places like that for med schools.
The weather though... has not been great this year. I love the weather and environment for 6 months out of the year, but Jan and Feb are very cold. It got to 5 degrees at one point. I have heard this has been an abnormal year though (apparently it was -20 yesterday in Wisconsin).
If you don't mind the cold, consider applying though! The fun, genius, and nerdy students that are your peers make it worthwhile :)

ai_summary: Central Michigan University has a friendly and supportive atmosphere that many students appreciate, highlighting the staff's commitment to their success and well-being. Academically, the faculty is noted for being approachable and invested in students, fostering strong relationships and promoting hands-on learning experiences. While students enjoy the vibrant campus life and the various student organizations available, some reviews mention a need for improvement in dining options and campus housing. The university offers a range of programs, including unique majors and a strong media program, but some students feel challenges with diversity and engagement within the town of Mount Pleasant. Overall, CMU is recognized for its welcoming community and opportunities for involvement, although some desire enhancements in communication and resources related to student services and campus amenities.

reviews.text: I have not attended central Michigan yet, only accepted but from what I experienced being on there campus in the fall, they have a very good student life and academics.
Honestly I love it there! The community I've found (which is a lot of nerds and artists) is so fulfilling, and I have already made so many friends
I like the friendships and connections you make here. I like my program specifically because of the teacher. But I would like you see some newer dorms. I would also like to see food open later.

My overall experience with CMU is great. The pace is amazing; the advisors are very informative and help you navigate any struggles. The financial aid department is a bit on the slower end; they do not communicate well via email. The professors are highly qualified and very responsive; I feel the material is easy to understand even if it's a bit more challenging.
Coming in as a freshman, there was always something to do on campus. Going out nearly every day of the week, shopping, nature walks, joining Greek life. It was just all great.

I really like my experience at Central Michigan University so far because the campus has a great vibe and most people are super welcoming, which made me excited to go there. But honestly, some of the professors are pretty hard to understand, and the food isn’t that good, so that’s definitely something I’d love to see improve. Overall, it’s a solid experience, just with a few things that could be better.
I feel like nothing, but everything is going on here all the time. if you want to have fun you are going to have to search for it and not live in your dorm.
It’s been great I’m excited to see how these next 4 years will play out an what opportunities the school with give me taking these classes

For this being my first year at central, I am loving it so far. I was super nervous about being thousands of miles away from home but honestly I've made so many friends and I've even connected with some of my professors too. Its been amazing.
My experience has been overall positive. One thing that I wish would change is how hard it is to work with campus offices when contacting them.

I have really enjoyed my time at Central Michigan University because it is a great home away from home and community for me to be a part of and to meet new people. It has also pushed me to get out of my comfort zone and become more of myself and change myself for the better.

Central Michigan University is known for producing successful teachers and educators. All of my professors so far have been delightful and have explained information in an easy-to-understand way. I enjoy all of my classes and I really like the environment CMU creates for its students.

I have thoroughly enjoyed my time here at Central so far. My biggest issues are with the incredibly slow wait times for financial aid and making payments, and with the costs of living, tuition, and housing/dining. It is too expensive. The level of support available does not meet the pace at which prices are increasing.

My time at Central has been a wild journey, from major changes to financial problems, but it was never anything that Central wasn't willing to help with. my time there has taught me that networking is key, its's not always about what you know but rather who you know and who you've tried to make genuine connections with throughout your time.
My overall experience at Central Michigan I would say is good classes aren't too hard but also challenging. Everything is relatively close or a walk away, everyone is relatively nice and inclusive.

ai_summary: Chapman University receives a mix of enthusiastic endorsements and constructive critiques from students. Many appreciate the close-knit community and small class sizes , which allow students to form personal relationships with professors and receive individualized attention. The Dodge College of Film and Media Arts is often highlighted for its hands-on learning opportunities and skilled instructors, while other academic programs, like business and theater, also receive positive mention. Student life is vibrant, with ample opportunities for involvement in clubs and activities, though some note a strong influence of Greek life and a somewhat cliquey social scene.
