Of course the amount of students in the classroom doesn't help at the moment to interact with the professors. The food they serve is delicious, diverse and the meal plan make it cheap. The only problem about the campus is that isn't specifically safe after 11:00 pm. But at least we receive emails every time that any incident happen, even though those incidents doesn't affect students.

I’m an older, non-traditional student who never imagined an Ivy League education was within my reach. Despite my age, the social student life experience has been incredible. With numerous student groups and a wide range of interests and cultural experiences, I find numerous ways to relax between the demanding rigor of classes. The opportunities for networking with alumni, companies, and others are endless. While Columbia does have its institutional and bureaucratic challenges, which are to be expected for any institution of its age, the education and opportunities are of the highest quality. I’m incredibly proud to be a Columbia Lion.
Columbia is a great school overall, even though recently there has been a lot of upheaval and overall division. I think generally the student body has a good view of each other and their programs.

ai_summary: At Concordia University Irvine, many students appreciate the close-knit and friendly campus atmosphere , often describing it as a small community where faculty and peers know one another personally. The small class sizes facilitate meaningful connections with professors , who are generally regarded as caring and committed to student success . However, some students express concerns about the administration, particularly regarding the financial aid office, academic advising, and limited diversity on campus . While the academics receive high praise for rigor and engagement, students often mention that campus life can feel lacking, with fewer on-campus activities and a subdued social scene . The university's Christ-centered culture and location near various attractions in Orange County are frequently highlighted as key positives. Overall, students value the education and personal growth opportunities provided by Concordia, although some aspects of student life and administration require improvement.

reviews.text: This is one of the best universities that is able to build not just academically, but entirely: emotionally, physically, and intellectually. Through its training in competence, connection building, and character building, one walks away having a strong foundation to go out and change the world for the good. To be a positive influence wherever one stands.

Although I haven’t officially started at Concordia University Irvine yet, my experience so far has been very positive. From the moment I began the application process, the staff and admissions team have been incredibly supportive and responsive. The school’s faith-based values and emphasis on compassion, service, and academic excellence really stood out to me.
I’m excited to join a community that promotes both personal and professional growth, especially in the nursing program. Based on my interactions, I already feel welcomed and confident that Concordia will provide the structure and encouragement I need to succeed.
If I could suggest one thing, it would be to make more virtual information sessions available for incoming students, especially those who haven’t started yet but want to feel more connected early on.
Your journey is always guided by professionals and a faith based campus, smaller classrooms for more support.
CUI has a good community. It's very small overall in terms of how many people attend. It does not fit the typical college experience you would expect.

I only had to visit Concordia University Irvine once to decide it was for me. For starters, the campus is GORGEOUS. It's on top of a hill with a beautiful view overlooking the town and neighborhoods below. Concordia also has a wonderful community filled with friendly people. It's not a super small school per say, but everyone still knows each other. It is like having friends everywhere you go. As for academics, while the community and campus are Concordia's stunning achievement, this school has also given me everything I need to succeed in the career of my choice. Thank you, Concordia!

I've visited Concordia University Irvine a few times, and every time I’m impressed by how beautiful the campus is. It has a peaceful and clean atmosphere that makes it a nice place to be. That said, it can feel a bit empty at times—I do wish I had more opportunities for in-person classes, as that would make the experience feel more connected and engaging.
One thing I really appreciate is how easy it is to get in touch with the staff. Everyone I’ve interacted with has been helpful, responsive, and supportive. Overall, the school is amazing and provides a great environment for learning. With more in-person interaction, it could be even better!

Some things that I really like about this this school are the many places outside to sit and gather your thoughts as well as do homework and study, the food options in the cafeteria, and the many mental health awareness options that are given by the school, such as walk-in therapy session and dog therapy.

The campus is beautfil, but currently under extensive construction the last two years and is "projected" to be done in 2027. School claims to be in debt of 6.5 million dollars and cut multiple athletics programs with no warning, after NCAA transfer portal was already closed. The classes are very hyper-specific to Concordia, claiming a blend of [insert course subject] and the christian beliefs, making curriculum not entirely focused on the actual subject and more how it relates to christianity.

As a nursing student at Concordia University Irvine, we have a different experience from the rest of the campus. Though I am in a separate campus, Concordia University Irvine holds a prestige and level of academics that shape individuals to become well-educated individuals.

Perfect school is you are looking for a loving environment with people who genuinely care about one another. It is an easy place to make friends especially because the classes are a lot smaller! Learning and getting to know students and professors makes academic life way easier!
I have enjoyed online classes and it feels engaging even from a distance. The students are well connected even outside of class.

Concordia University Irvine is set in the beautiful area of Irvine, a school dedicated to the values that shaped Western Civilization and developing wise, honorable, and cultivated citizens over men who desire nothing but monetary gain.
So far my interactions with the professors have been great! There are a lot of resources that the school offers.

I am currently a senior in high school but will be attending the University when I am a Freshman in college. When taking a campus tour I really loved how serene and small Concordia was. When walking around I really was able to envision myself there for the next for 4 years to complete my academics along with playing collegiate level baseball.

The teaching credential program at Concordia University Irvine is very thorough. I feel they are providing me with the necessary tools to complete the program successfully. This program is compatible with a full time 9-5 job until you reach the student teaching portion.

ai_summary: Concordia University Wisconsin (CUW) offers a close-knit , supportive atmosphere with a strong Christian community that many students appreciate. The professors are consistently noted for their approachability and dedication to student success, often fostering personal connections in small class sizes that enhance learning. The campus is described as beautiful, especially with its views of Lake Michigan and an underground tunnel system that helps avoid harsh winter weather. However, several reviews mention issues with the campus food quality and limited diversity , with some expressing dissatisfaction with student life and weekend activities. Overall, CUW is seen as a good option for those prioritizing academics and a tight community, although it may not suit those looking for a vibrant social scene or a more diverse environment.

reviews.text: I have had an overall great experience at CONCORDIA UNIVERSITY WISCONSIN my freshman year. I love the Christian centered education and campus life so I am able to stay connected in my faith. The professors are amazing, they are supportive and helpful. I like the free tutoring especially for my anatomy class. I academic resources are so helpful everyone wants to see me succeed. I also like the campus events that are hosted. They are a good break away from studying. I would like to see a change in the food options on campus. I wish there were more healthier options available.

I like how the professor are. They want to help you succeed and the campus is beautiful with being able to look out at the lake the football program is amazing and the dorms are a nice experience I am happy that I’ve came here and hope I can stay here and play football.

Academics and sense of community was cultivated if you were apart of the Lutheran church; limited social opportunities outside of this. Felt like a glorified high school, I regret paying thousands of dollars in religious education courses that were required for my science-based undergraduate degree.

Recruited for soccer and was told they could use a player like me. 2 years of playing and was never on team roster of 22. Just recruited to help keep program funded as they keep pooling 40 players. Concordia has the highest student athlete ratio. This is because they have too many athletes on a money grab to pay the bills. Otherwise University was financially struggling due to Chancellor letters of financial distress. Tuition was $36k annually and we were a money grab. Don't commit to Coach Seleska unless you have a commitment in writing. He doesn't recruit well and just tells you what you want to hear so you will sign. Then he waits till training camp to take players from a large pool of 40. You could just go to any other state D3 school and pay 1/4 the price for a better education on a bigger campus with a better soccer program. He has run it into a losing program with no strategy. There is nothing to do on campus other than sleep in your dorm room. No one even stays the weekends.

I wish I had more of a positive experience with the school like everyone else I knew had with their school but frankly that wasn't the case. An important factor to remember when choosing this school is that it's a religious institution first, school second, when it should be the other way around, they only care about what the LCMS has to say in which they have no experience running a business rather than listening to business experts as to how the school is ran (true story). Don't get me started on the people, nearly everyone I've met at CUW has an "I'm still stuck in high school" mentality which in return, creates a hostile environment of some of the most judgemental people you'll ever meet.. especially if you don't identify as religious (and it's okay if you don't). I was also harassed and stalked by two of the football players and the school did nothing to protect me and other girls in my shoes and allowed them to stay on campus. Please please please research this before you come

I really love it here at CUW! The professors genuinely care about their students and go above and beyond what could be reasonably expected of them in order to help us be the best version of ourselves. The environment around campus is so kind and welcoming, and there is almost always something going on around campus to go. Our Campus Safety officers are extremely kind, and their constant presence really allows me to feel truly safe and at home. The location could not be more perfect! Being right on Lake Michigan is such a blessing - you can always go hang out by the lake and be reminded of how cool God is! The Mequon area is extremely nice, and everything you could need is within a few miles. We are far enough out of Milwaukee to not feel like we're in the big city but also close enough that going to Milwaukee for shopping, games, events, or anything else is not a hassle. I would highly recommend CUW!

Concordia is a good school if you really want to focus on school. The class sizes are small, so you are sure to make close relationships with your professors and other classmates. The downside is that the diversity is quite small.
My experience at Concordia has been great. The people, sports and environment is full of excitement. I like how everyone is so connected and helpful. It makes you feel at home

CUW has been a great place for me and my next step in life. I have enjoyed my experience very much so far. My team and friends have been a big part of my life and I pray that that continues for the next 3 years. The campus is beautiful and I love the small population size and everything CUW has to offer.
I like most of the professors that teach the OT program. I would like to see the amount of busy work change to be more productive and successful in my learning journey.

So far, Concordia University is great. We have visited four times and everything seems to be in order. Polite, friendly staff and coaches there to help you achieve your future educational and athletic goals.
I have enjoyed my time so far at Concordia. I like that it is a small campus. It is very nice because it is right on the lake. Not much to do in the surrounding area.
I am enjoying my experience at Concordia University. The instructors are very supportive and thorough. I would like to go in person not on line.
My experience with Concordia University has been positive, specifically in athletics, academics, and faith.

My first year at Concordia was awesome. My professors very approachable and generous when it came to there time and assignemnt in there class. The Campus by far is onbe of the best parts. you cant beat looking off the bluff at Lake Michigan everyday.

ai_summary: Cornell University is frequently praised for its beautiful campus and extensive academic opportunities , fostering a balanced "work-hard play-hard" environment. Many students appreciate the diverse community and the wealth of resources available to explore various interests. While academics are challenging and rigorous, students often highlight the dedication of professors and the stimulating coursework that prepares them for future endeavors. However, some express concerns about the high cost of attendance and the competitive atmosphere, which can lead to stress and isolation if one doesn’t find their niche. The social scene is vibrant, with plenty of extracurricular activities and events, although some feel that involvement in Greek life is often emphasized. Overall, students recognize that while Cornell has its challenges, it offers a distinctive and enriching college experience.

reviews.text: Office of Academic Discovery and Impact, and my major advisor are the main reasons I was able to succeed during my undergraduate career. Finding your support system is the most important thing to have a great experience, in my opinion. Times may get tough, but a support system is invaluable to keep moving forward.

I appreciate all of the resources available to students and have had wonderful professors. I don't love paying for laundry, having to pay for a bus pass after year one, or the hours of the dining halls (earliest they open is 7, most close by 8:30).

Overall, my experience has been really great. I feel very safe on campus and love all the academic and health opportunities like the tutors, libraires, courses, and gyms, dining options and walkability. However, it is not financially comforting to come here as tuition is awfully expensive and not very realistic.

Cornell is a great place for the arts and sciences academically. Its housing could be improved and tuition/financial aid needs to be more clear. However, there are a lot of awesome student-run organizations where I've found my place.

We like Ezra Cornell promised “any person … any study” to students of the University he founded.
What could be improved- Cornell teaches the principles of Kaizen to strive for continuous improvement.

Although chemical science was my primary aspiration, my experience at Cornell was made completely through music. Playing in Cornell's Chamber Orchestra, for instance, kept my musical interests as a violist thriving. In addition, I was able to embrace my Chinese background at Cornell, whether that be through the Chinese Students Association, whose hot pot nights were genuinely too good to pass up, or the Cornell-China and Asia-Pacific Studies (CAPS) Program, where I gained invaluable internship experience studying abroad in Beijing. Overall, Cornell helped me solidify the kind of impact I hope to make on the world, both as a scientist and a student.

I transferred to Cornell my sophomore year. I thought the Office of Undergraduate Biology did a good job at integrating their transfer students. My overall experience was pretty good; classes were interesting, the campus was nice, there were lots of clubs/organizations to join, and the food was good! There are not a ton of dining options on central campus - they're mostly by the dorms which are more peripheral. You'll get your steps in for sure :D It does get very grey in the winter, but when the sun comes out, you'll see EVERYONE outside on the slope or on the quad trying to soak up the rays. I found lots of awesome study spots around campus in random buildings - one of my favorite places to study was MVR. The surrounding nature is amazing. I have to recommend walking around campus with no destination in mind! The Commons was a nice area with lots of good food. Apple Fest down in the Commons was one of the best fall activities!

My favorite aspect of Cornell was its location in a small town. Being somewhat isolated made it much easier to build a close-knit community, and I felt more connected to the people around me than I would have in a large city. I also appreciated how flexible the curriculum was — I was able to take classes across different colleges and explore interests outside my primary field, which made my academic experience feel more personalized and well-rounded.

Overall, it's a pretty good environment that provides the things you need for your future and present. School is pretty big and definitely has some issues of its own but I would say its pros easily overcome the cons.

Overall good experience, but very expensive. I spent a lot of time working in order to pay for it, which affected my grades and overall experience at the school. I also met lifelong friends and received opportunities I never would have elsewhere.

Cornell University has been an incredibly rewarding place to start my college journey. As a Black freshman football player studying biological sciences, I expected a challenging workload, and my first semester definitely pushed me academically. I finished with a 3.0 GPA, which I am proud of, especially balancing academics and athletics. One of the highlights of my experience has been the support from my professors, who are approachable, understanding, and genuinely willing to help students succeed. I’ve also loved the variety of food options on campus, the party scene, the diversity of the student body, and the strong sense of community among students. Being part of the football team has helped me build close relationships and feel connected to campus life. However, I would like to see improved meal rotation in the dining halls and renovations to some freshman dorms. Overall, Cornell has been a challenging yet fulfilling environment that has helped me grow academically and personally.

As an animal science student, Cornell has a lot of great resources and programs in their department. Lots of research opportunities and hands-on experiences for students. Ithaca is nicest in the summer when nobody else is here.

The academics are amazing at Cornell, professors are some of the best I've ever had. The large lecture classes are a bit disengaging but the discussion classes and smaller upper level courses make up for it. I would like to see smaller classes though even at lower levels implemented throughout a variety of majors and subjects. The food is okay, much better than much other college cafeteria food, and large in diversity, but lacking in terms of healthiness and taste sometimes. My dorm as a transfer is not as nice as I would hope, having been a recently converted frat house that was just recently underwent renovation this summer but many of my friends have much nicer dorms with in-house laundry and dining halls.
pretty good! the experience has been very enriching and i have gone to europe with the chorus and sang in an acapella group

I love Cornell University. From my professors, to the people who help at resource centers, to Cornell Health, everyone here is committed to supporting and lifting up students. The campus and city is beautiful.

ai_summary: Creighton University offers a supportive community with passionate professors who are dedicated to student success, making the academic experience enjoyable, particularly in fields like nursing and business. The small class sizes foster strong relationships between students and faculty, allowing for personalized attention. However, some students express concerns about housing shortages and the quality of dining options, which can detract from the overall experience. The campus life is vibrant with numerous available clubs and sports, though students report that social activities can feel limited, especially for those under 21. The emphasis on Jesuit values and community service enriches the cultural environment, but there are criticisms regarding diversity and inclusion efforts. While the university provides ample resources for academic and personal growth, some students feel that financial aid and administrative support could be improved. Overall, Creighton stands out for its close-knit environment and commitment to holistic education, but issues like cost and resource allocation merit consideration for prospective students.

reviews.text: Creighton offers great academic opportunites and various programs beginning freshman year. They offer clubs and activities on campus such as Model UN, quadball, e sports, intramurals, or Greek life that truly add to the community and positive envirnoment here. I was abroad my first semester freshman year, and while it was a unique path, the staff and systems at creighton aided me and my academics to be extremely happy and successful. The only complaint I have includes the housing. Students were overadmitted and the housing could not take them, so I had to live in sub par conditions without any assistance from Creighton staff my freshman year and was placed in a similar situation sophomore year as I was "forgot to be housed". I eventually got the success center to solve the issue, but it took months of communication and refused requests. Overall, Omaha is lovely and I feel safe and excited to explore campus and the city everday. There is much to do and the area attracts great people!
not very good for commuters, i liked how many clubs there were, some professors care about your success a lot whereas some could care less

I liked how has small class sizes. The small class sizes allow you to get to know your professors. There is also a really good student success center to make sure students are able to be successful in their classes. Another bonus is that Creighton has good athletics and lots of school spirit. I would like to see more food options. There is limited food options to grab if you have to eat in-between classes.

My experience at Creighton University has been very positive and rewarding. As a student in the business school, I have been particularly impressed with the strong finance program they offer. The professors are not only highly qualified but also approachable and friendly, which creates an encouraging learning environment. One of the biggest advantages for me has been the relatively small class sizes, typically ranging from 15 to 30 students. This allows for meaningful interaction, personalized attention, and a great overall classroom experience. Additionally, the community here is wonderful, the people are great and supportive, making my time at Creighton both academically fulfilling and personally enjoyable.

Personally, I was disappointed with my experience at Creighton University. The academics are solid, however, I never felt like I belonged. The "friends" that I thought I made pretended like I didn't exist once I moved to an off-campus apartment in Omaha. And for my last two years at Creighton I was a loner. I unfortunately cannot recommend Creighton University of you want a fun and close knit college community.
So far awesome experience and I look forward to a full experience. I’m thankful for the opportunity and can’t wait to meet my professors and make new connections.

Creighton is a very safe and sound environment for college students, considering every student goes through a huge transition from high school to college. Students tackle the change by navigating through the shift in class workload to living by themselves in dormitories and being on their own. I believe Creighton definitely provides a safe space to grow, learn, and tackle these challenges with excellent support.However, I believe I have seen students discuss and worry mostly about finances, especially the tuition and cost of attendance, which tends to be higher than other colleges in the USA. I believe it often serves as an added stress factor for students, which hinders their growth and comfortability while being away from home. Moreover, I believe Creighton definitely needs more marketing targeted toward international students to increase diversity, as I believe the exchange of cultures is one of the major aspects when it comes to the college experience.

My experience at Creighton has been incredibly formative and life changing, both personally and professionally. As a Jesuit institution, Creighton truly lives out its values and support principles and values that I live by on a daily basis. What I liked most about Creighton was the strong sense of community which makes Creighton feel more than just a school. The smaller class size allowed for more personal engagement and meaningful experiences. The one thing that I would change is while support services exist, increasing awareness and accessibility of mental health and academic resources could make a big difference.
I would die for Creighton’s CSDJ.(Department of Computer Science, Design, and Journalism) The professors and the classes in this department have absolutely captured my heart.
I have had a really good experience at Creighton so far. Campus Ministry has particularly been rewarding for me as I have participated in many retreats and opportunities set by them.

Creighton is a great school with a great campus and academics. The small school environment helps you focus on discovering your personal identity and focus on education. The student life is very inclusive and not very overwhelming. My only complaint about this school is how expensive it is and the lack of diversity. I do not like how the admissions department panders to wealthy suburban applicants.

Creighton University’s professors are all so immensely passionate about the subjects they teach, which makes the learning process far more enjoyable. You’ll have to take a variety of courses in different subjects due to the Magis Core requirements, meaning you’ll get to discern areas of interest for majors and minors.

Overall Creighton has been a great experience for me. I have always felt supported accademically and mentally. One thing that I would critique about the University is that recently they haven't taken into account the amount of students they are accepting into the school. This didn't concern me my freshman year, but going into my sophmore year I started to have more trouble with things like housing. Creighton overall has pretty good housing, but not enough space for everyone. For example, I am living in "McGloin" the only take is that I am not living in a room, I am living in a converted louge space with 3 other girls. Other than that, I love that Creighton offers free sporting events and will also have random free events on campus.

They are definitely academic focused which was great for me but the diversity of the student body was lacking and even though they advertise that it’s not a Catholic school that forces religion on you, you’re required to take religion classes. There’s also a major housing crisis because they admitted more students than they have rooms for and what dorms they do have are very unequal.
I’m absolutely so thankful for Creighton, and the strive to be good for their students. I really appreciate the effort Creighton puts into 1st Generation, Low-Income students.

ai_summary: D'Youville University offers a unique experience characterized by its small class sizes and close relationships between students and professors, which many reviews highlight as a significant advantage. While some students appreciate the supportive faculty and strong academic programs, particularly in health professions, several reviews point out frustrations with campus life, noting a lack of events and inadequate student services. The campus is described as cozy and friendly , but some find it isolating with limited extracurricular activities and poor dining options. Tuition costs have been a recurring concern, as many express dissatisfaction with rising fees and feel that the quality of some services doesn't align with the expenses. Overall, students appreciate the community feel at D'Youville but are divided on administrative effectiveness and student engagement opportunities.

reviews.text: Located in a not so good part of downtown Buffalo, as a young female you can feel a little nervous walking campus. Parking situation is horrific due to being in the middle of the city. The few parking lots we had they decided to take one and build a new building on it. Professors are barely knowledgeable enough to teach us what we need to know. I ended up teaching myself for the most part.

The campus is beautiful. So many updates, clean well maintained. For my first year I may have taken on to many courses. I do appreciated the student success center which is always available for extra help. Overall great school.

I like that D’Youville University offers online and self-paced learning, which makes it convenient to balance school with other responsibilities. The flexibility really helps students manage their time effectively. However, sometimes things feel a bit unorganized or still in development, with changes happening unexpectedly. I think more consistency and clearer communication would make the experience even better overall.

D'Youville College has been a strong foundation for my nursing career, providing me with knowledge, confidence, support I need to be successful in this field. From the very beginning, I was welcomed into a diverse and inclusive environment where students from all backgrounds are valued and respected. This diversity has broadened my perspective and deepened my ability to connect with patients from all walks of life. The faculty at D'Youville are not only experts in their field, but also genuinely committed to student success. I've received personalized guidance, mentorship, and encouragement every step of the way. The rigorous academic curriculum challenged me to think critically and grow both personally and professionally. Through hands-on clinical experiences and strong classroom instruction, D'Youville has prepared me to become a compassionate, skilled nurse ready to make a difference. I'm proud to say this college has shaped the nurse I'm becoming.

They seem unorganized when trying to communicate their own policies.
Also campus safety is not helpful

I enjoy the area of D’youville as well as the diversity in the school . The student success center is very helpful and I believe that I will be able to get the support I need to be successful in school .

I love the environment here at D’Youville University and you will love it too especially if you prefer a smaller school community. You can make a lot of connections here and since the campus is small it makes it easier to get to know others. I like that the class sizes are smaller it makes it easier to focus in class and engage with your peers and professors. There have been issues with a dorm building because construction from the city around the school caused a few gas leaks and power outages. The way the campus handled the situation was not my favorite, but they handled it. Academics and campus environment are the best parts here on campus and make me like the school.
Went to nursing school 🏫. Received a quality education was able to pass my boards on the first try. Highly recommend

Honestly, if you're thinking about going to D’Youville..
. don’t. I’m saying this as someone who actually went and kept hoping things would get better n they didn’t.
First off, there’s no campus life. It’s so dead. No events, barely any clubs or student involvement, and it just feels super isolating. If you’re hoping for a real college experience, this isn’t it.
Student services are useless. They ignore or pass u around between departments with no real answers. Don’t expect much help with financial aid, advising, or even just basic support.
Professors? Bro they literally act like they’re out to catch you slipping instead of actually teaching. Feedback is vague or rude, and it feels like you're constantly having to teach yourself everything. If you fall behind even a little, don’t expect anyone to care or help you get back on track.
The worst part? You’re paying a ton for this experience.
If you have other options, seriously, take them. Save yourself the stress.

I like everything
I like to study
It’s good school
They are my people who can help you
Most of them are science

Overall my experience through D'Youville has by far excelled my expectations. The students and staff are friendly and accommodating. Party scene is not large but you will build a connection with everyone on campus.

Overall, I have had a great experience at D'Youville. There are many school events that are done all throughout both semesters. I have had some amazing professors over the years. The classrooms, athletic facilities, and dining facilities are newly remodeled and very modern.

As a transfer student athlete, there have been many positives about D'Youville. Maggie and Saint are the best! Academically, it is rigiorus, but the professors are supportive of the athletes and the travel that goes with it. It is a small campus but that makes for a strong student community. My residence hall was newly renovated but there was not a lot of storage and there were a few unfinished things when we moved in. The school does a good job of setting up activities, especially in the beginning of the year, for the students like Redfest a celebration of D'Youville complete with bands and amusement park rides.

I like D'youville University because it has small classes, and the professors know the students by name. The professors are helpful and always there when students need help. The school has a good nursing program where it prepares its students for their careers. As an undergrad, I was able to find a job after I completed undergrad, and most of the hospitals in Buffalo love D'youville students, according to them, the students are well fit for careers when they come from D'youville.

DYU has an amazing athletics program which I am able to be apart of. I also love how small the campus is, I feel as though each of my professors are able to connect with me and help me with my academics. I have really enjoyed being at DYU.

ai_summary: Daemen University is characterized by its small campus and supportive academic environment , allowing for personalized attention from professors who genuinely care about student success. Many reviews highlight the close-knit community , with small class sizes fostering intimate connections among students and faculty. While the academic programs, especially in healthcare and sciences, are noted for their rigor and preparation for future careers, the campus life has room for improvement, as students often mention a lack of activities and social opportunities, particularly on weekends. Some students express concerns about the dining options, outdated facilities, and parking issues. Overall, Daemen is seen as a good fit for students focused on their studies who appreciate a quieter , more academic-oriented environment .

reviews.text: I enjoyed the environment and education I received at Daemen University. I am currently an incoming medical student, and I feel I was adequately prepared for my future medical education.

Daemen University was the undergraduate school that I transferred to after Medaille shut down. I greatly enjoyed my time at Daemen, meeting new friends and amazing professors who gave me constructive criticism for me to improve. I wish I had started my college career at Daemen, but I am glad to have had the short experience at Medaille first, to see the differences between the two colleges. One of the things I enjoyed most at Daemen was that they immediately let me get experience for my major (childhood education/special education) and placed me in classrooms right away. I also enjoyed being able to come to my advisor, professors, or other members of the education staff with questions and concerns. I enjoyed having a closer community as Daemen is a smaller campus, but the only problem with that was that there was very limited parking. I feel like there could have been sections of lots reserved for specific year students or majors to help with the small lot sizes.

Daemen Nursing Program is a joke, they lack all sorts of support, they are incompetent with teaching some of their higher level classes and the teachers are unprofessional allow some students to cheat and the honest ones they fail out in attempts to take the Federal Funded money over and over, I spent over 100,000 dollars at this school and left with 2 unfinished degrees, THAT IS A REFLECTION OF THIS PROGRAM AND THE LACK OF CARE TO GET PEOPLE IN THE RIGHT PROGRAMS AND GRADUATE THEM, they fail out 80%of the NP students and often keep them for 3 years to steal the Federal backed money .They nursing teacher are incompetent they do things like teach advanced pathophysiology when they are not qualified to teach anatomy. all the teacher need to feel ashamed at their own lack of competence. I worked so hard for 6 years, and it amounted to NOTHING but 100000 in debt accruing interested due to this very bad unethical school and program
Daemen is a small and tight-knit community. I feel like after a semester I already knew a majority of students in my courses.

I majored in animation, so I can't speak for the majority of the health and sciences student population. Personally, I quite enjoyed attending Daemen. The professors were wonderful, understanding, and very knowledgeable in their subject(s).

Great environment. Things did become stressful toward the end but help was all around, which is one of the things I love about this school. It’s in a very convenient location as far as stores, fun and even making friends and networking.
Daemen is a great, fun place to learn! The community is amazing, and I highly recommend it to anyone. They have amazing clubs, professors, and athletic teams.

During my tour at Daemen University, I had a great first impression. The campus was clean, easy to navigate, and had a welcoming feel. I liked seeing the academic buildings and learning about the personalized education they offer. The focus on career prep and internships stood out, and the strong sense of community was clear. Since I’m interested in being recruited for volleyball, I was excited to meet Coach Kaysie. She’s new but spoke highly of the program’s success, including multiple NCAA tournament appearances and East Coast Conference titles. I wish we had seen more of the residence halls, as some were only shown in photos. It also would’ve been helpful to hear from more students in different majors. Overall, the tour made me feel confident that Daemen could be a great fit!
I loved going to Daemen for my undergraduate degree and cannot wait to start in the fall for my masters. They are amazing for getting you job opportunities and academic opportunities!

I would like for the campus to change how they do housing and how they handle problems when you have one with roommates. Many of the students are in a click and they will bully you if you are not a part of their click. If you have a group project most of the students in my experience will not help you with that said project. Sometimes you get a good partner but not always. Many of the students will not study with you or do any social activities. Overall most interactions at Daemen are negative ones.
I really enjoyed Daemen. It was my first year as a transfer student there. They were super helpful with the transition there and guiding/advising me.

I am having a great experience with my studies at Daemen University! The Professors are amazing and always ready to help guide me when needed. All questions and concerns are addressed quickly over email and taken care of.

I am an older student. I had to ask for help from the Dimitrovski of the disability department and she was unhelpful, and she did not seem to want to even try to do her job. I was thoroughly disappointed. This department did to want to get to work to discuss the makeup of the class. She would not even provide me with a chair I could comfortably sit in. I was very disappointed with my stats professor, I have never had a teacher who wanted to teach less.I have had several great nursing instructors, and all my classes are online classes. I am no stranger to colleges, I have attended many different schools over the years while earning multiple degrees. This college feels very immature and not suited for adult students. This campus is small and parking is horrible here. I can't wait for this year to be up to transfer into a real university. This place is like a community college, and don't come here if you have any disabilities. You won't get help from anyone.

I would like to see a variety in dining options to make the price increase of the meal plans worth it. I would also like to see more campus apartments be built instead of shoving everyone to Collegiate Village when there is no more room for upperclassman.
Daemen is always providing events for their students outside of class. The professors are usually easy to get in touch with and quick to respond.

ai_summary: At Dallas Baptist University, many reviews highlight the strong sense of community and the emphasis on a Christ-centered education. Students appreciate the small class sizes and the personal attention they receive from professors, who are often described as caring and invested in their students' success. The campus itself is frequently praised for its beauty and safety , creating a welcoming atmosphere. While many enjoy the activities and traditions that foster a sense of belonging, some express a desire for more diverse events and enhanced student services, particularly concerning housing and financial aid processes. Academically, students find value in the diverse program offerings, although there are comments about the high tuition costs and occasional frustrations with administrative support. Overall, DBU is viewed as a nurturing environment for both spiritual and personal growth, appealing to students who prioritize faith in their academic journey.

reviews.text: The school has an overall welcoming and loving environment. The religious aspect is involved in almost everything you do in and outside of the classroom. For those who are looking to strengthen their faith and relationship with God, I highly recommend taking a tour and seeing how you like it.
They changed my life for the better and I can’t wait to go back in the spring and they’re so respectful
I am glad they gave me an opportunity to continue my education. I was skeptical but the advisor made it easy and put the on the right path.

My experience with Dallas Baptist University was life changing. The commitment of the campus community and leadership to a Christ-centered college experience goes beyond just the name or history of the institution. It is evident in the every day experience through academics, student organizations, athletics, and so on. The mission of DBU is to prepare Christian servant leaders for all fields - and it is clear that this mission drives decision making, how faculty and staff do their jobs, and all campus initiatives. All classes regardless of academic discipline are taught through a Christian worldview, using the Bible as the standard of truth. This is a great place for students to not only receive an excellent education, but to grow spiritually and be prepared to stand firm as a Christian leader in their respective callings.

I love this school! You get what you pay for and it lives up to what they advertise! Dallas Baptist University is more than a college—it’s a community that truly feels like home. As a student, I’ve experienced how DBU combines academic excellence with genuine spiritual growth. Professors don’t just teach; they mentor and pray with us, helping us discover our purpose through Christ-centered education. The campus overlooks beautiful rolling hills and the Dallas skyline, creating the perfect environment for reflection and connection. Whether it’s worship nights, service projects, or late-night study sessions in the library, every moment at DBU feels intentional. Here, faith and learning intertwine, shaping not only our minds but also our hearts for service.

My overall experience at His Baptist University has been both inspiring and transformative. The environment here emphasizes not only academic excellence but also spiritual growth and personal integrity. The instructors genuinely care about each student’s success and take time to connect on a deeper level, which has strengthened my faith and confidence.
I’ve grown academically, emotionally, and spiritually since enrolling. Balancing school, work, and family hasn’t always been easy, but the support from peers has encouraged me to keep going and trust that my purpose is bigger than my challenges. Dallas Baptist University has truly become a place that helps me walk boldly in my calling and continue developing as a leader in faith and service.

My experience in DBU has been amazing! As an international freshman, I was extremely nervous about starting college, especially since I'm in an entirely new country all by myself, but the welcoming environment of DBU made my transformation easier. I can see that the professors genuinely care about helping students grow both academically and spiritually. I've also enjoyed being a part of a Christ-centered community where faith is integrated in everything we do, and I've made a couple of friends who shares the same belief and faith. Aside from those things, the campus itself is beautiful! There are tons of sceneries that are Instagram-picture-worthy. So, overall, DBU has started to feel like home, and I'm excited to see how I'll continue to grow here over the next few years.

Dallas Baptist University is the absolute best. No matter what we do, Christ is the center of it all. Every class is centered around Christ, and leads back to the Bible. Chapel three times a week is such an amazing start to my days and we always have the best speakers. Every professor and every staff member is so intentional with their students. It is just such an amazing environment to be in. Even working in admissions as a student worker, all of the staff is so encouraging, uplifting, and just so fun to work with and makes me want to come to work everyday. Just everything about DBU is so amazing, and it is all because everything we do is for Him!

We are far from perfect but if you're looking for Christ-centered community and biblically based education, I highly recommend Dallas Baptist University. DBU is a beautiful campus focused on educating students in fields of public speaking, critical thinking, and decision making centered around a biblical worldview. I have loved being a student at DBU. The community is unmatchable. Fellow students and professors are believers in Christ, ready to walk through life with you and guide you to the Lord daily.

DBU has been such a blessing to me and my family. From intentionality, generous financial aid, and personal connections, the staff at DBU has done everything to make me feel valued and at home. The size is perfectly fit so I am able to grow closer to my peers, while also being able to meet new people everyday. DBU gives you all the tools you need in order to be successful as a student and in the workplace. Students here are not just a number, they are brothers and sisters in Christ who share common goals, values, and aspirations.

Overall DBU has been a good school so far. There is a good community of people here who really care about each other. There is also always something to do as far as fun activities are concerned, like frisbee and pickleball and things like that.
I was not impressed with how I was told all my credits would transfer from another university (I am a transfer student) and then the degree plan changed. I was not informed about it and so I will have to take an extra semester because suddenly a lot of my credits don't work towards the degree anymore. Other than that and a disgusting housing situation, it has been good.

DBU has been excellent in developing me in my academics and spiritual life. The professors are so intentional and really value working with the students who want to be worked with. DBU as a whole values excellence and faith in everything anyone does, and it has been refreshing to see that culture saturate into the students. Most people here are very kind and welcoming, and create a space where anyone can thrive and be who they are, and grow as a Christian. There are also fun events going on all the time, and there are so many student organizations anyone could get involved with.

As a proud two-time alumnus of Dallas Baptist University, I can confidently say that my experience at DBU has been both life-shaping and deeply enriching. From my very first semester, it was evident that DBU is more than just a university—it is a Christ-centered community committed to developing servant leaders. Professors, staff, and fellow students consistently pointed me back to the hope of Christ, creating an environment where faith was not just acknowledged but integrated into every aspect of the college experience.
Equally important, DBU challenged me academically. The programs I completed were rigorous and intentional, equipping me with both the critical thinking and practical skills necessary to thrive in my career. Faculty members were not only knowledgeable experts in their fields but also mentors who cared deeply about my personal and spiritual growth.

My experience at Dallas Baptist University has been one of the most transformative times of my life already and this is my first year! I highly recommend it to everyone. I have been challenged as a student and as a man.

I LOVE DBU!!!! DBU is a place I truly consider to be home. I feel so seen and have always felt like it is the place where I belong! The Lord is so present at DBU and I consider it a true gift to be a part of it! The professors, students, and faculty are amazing! DBU is the place I always prayed for! :)

ai_summary: Dartmouth College offers a unique blend of rigorous academics and a tight-knit community, making it stand out among Ivy League institutions. Reviews highlight the exceptional quality of education , with small class sizes and dedicated professors who prioritize undergraduate learning, fostering an environment of collaboration rather than competition. The campus is celebrated for its beauty and outdoor opportunities, appealing to those who enjoy nature, though its rural location can feel isolating. The social scene often revolves around Greek life and campus events, which some find overwhelming if they don't align with mainstream activities. While many students appreciate the strong support systems and networks available, some challenges exist, particularly for those from less rigorous academic backgrounds, as they may feel a significant adjustment is needed. Overall, students express a deep sense of belonging and community, contributing to a transformative college experience .

reviews.text: You can find wonderful research and career opportunities in Dartmouth. Many professors are willing to do much to help struggling students and would like to get to know them better. Not all incoming students are used to the academic rigor, but there are supportive professors who can help. Although the people here are nice for the most part, there is still some racism or bullying within the community. This particularly concerns the Digital Communications Manager of the Hop; while coaching the crew team at her last school, she unfairly restricted active participation for certain students who didn't satisfy her biases, harshly punished them for minor rule-breaking that she herself and other students were not above doing and used it as an excuse to kick them off the team, and was also involved in slandering such students to get them in more trouble. Dartmouth should really do some research on the people they consider hiring and not just assume any of their alumni is a good person.

Very strong alumni network with amazing traditions such as the freshman trips and the bonfire/winter carnivals. Overall amazing once in a lifetime experience that I will remember before going back to the city.

Dartmouth is an incredibly special place! The level of commitment you get from your professors is unmatched by other institutions. Although it can feel small at times, it's size offers great benefits. It is very easy to win grants and other forms of funding for your any research, club, or event you want to pursue. The professors are nearly always open to get a meal with you, and genuinely care about your success. The student culture is very welcoming and friendly. No matter where you are on campus there is always somebody around that would love to learn your name and sit and chat. Every day is work hard, play hard. Us Dartmouth students are very committed to our studies and reaching our career goals, but at the same time we want to live it up—whether thats skiing at "The Dartmouth Skiway," going to a local farmers market, playing pong at one of the fraternities, or even taking a weekend road trip to Boston or Montreal.

Prospective of a parent/alum: there are lots of opportunities, clubs, frats/sororities, career etc. However, competition is the key word here. Every step of the way you have to compete and be better than the next in line. Meaningful Club? Need to prove you are the best. Not just: why don’t I try badminton or consulting? Doesn’t fly. Also true about frats: need to do lots of prep and KNOW in advance. Career opportunities are unparallel and the career office is super supportive. But you are fighting for a few amazing positions against a highly potent pool. Should be perfect. Bottom line: you fill fight the odds to get there, but competition will just intensify then.

I was really impressed with the professors especially, I never imagined that I could take a professor out to lunch or even talk and discuss their works. The research was always there always hands on, though the social scene was a bit hierarchical... But it was an unforgettable experience

I am currently a student at Dartmouth and I love it. It is very challenging and will test the resilience of its students, but the payoff is worth it. There truly is something for everyone here. The area surrounding the school is small but beautiful. Housing can be kind of a tossup--there are great residence halls and also some pretty bad ones. The food is generally good, but the options are limited. There is a group of people for everyone and I have met some truly amazing people here.
BEST SCHOOL EVER. I have never seen such well rounded individuals in every facet of life ever in my life! Regardless of the ranking Dartmouth is best.

COME TO DARTMOUTH!! Never in my life have I met such incredible, down to earth, loving people. As a non legacy non athlete on a full ride scholarship, I never felt alienated ONCE- people are so lovely here. There is a REASON I hear so many people choose here over MIT, Harvard, etc: we are actually happy here. Party life is amazing, academics are engaging and incredible, and I have never been happier.
Some advice!!!
1. BUY IN!! Dartmouth almost feels like a summer camp at first, complete with silly traditions, crazy dances, and pranks. It is SO MUCH FUN if you roll with it and completely buy in :)
2. Join things!!! I joined acapella here- best decision of my entire life. I have already made lifelong friends across all years!
3. Take it easy first term. I’m premed, but am only taking one STEM class in order to get used to the colleges pacing. Its manageable don’t worry!!
Can’t wait to see you on the green ❤️

It is a very lovely environment that facilitates higher learning. There are plenty of opportunities to join clubs, sports teams, and get internships throguht the college. Most of the professors are research professors so they understand the struggles studnets have to go through and are easy going as long as you talk to them. Greek life is great, very inclusive with gender inclusive housing options as well as the traditional fraternity and sorority. Networking optiosn are outstanding too with a lot of people that you can talk to about furthering your ideas on a profession

Dartmouth appeals to a specific type of person. As an Ivy League school, the College offers a lot of institutional funding for student's education, faculty, and experiences both on and off campus. The quaint New England setting is in one of the most picturesque parts of the country and it offers a kind of quiet that allows you to focus on your studies. If you're looking for a party school in a big city, this isn't it.

Dartmouth has more than earned its prestige as the school is wonderful in nearly all aspects. The professors are excellent and top of their field, the students are engaged and interesting, and just about every experience you could hope to have would be covered by the activities on offer.

As someone who didn't go to an elite private high school, the academics at Dartmouth have felt daunting. I have constantly felt behind compared to my peers and it is reflected in my grades, unfortunately. No matter how hard I study in certain science classes, I tend to fall either at or below median due to the fact that over 70% of them have a stronger background. Dartmouth's STEM classes are really not accessible for middle/lower class students. Most STEM professors are unhelpful. The social sciences at Dartmouth are phenomenal. As someone who is an Anthropology major, doing research in Anth, and premed, I have to say that the Anth Dept is supportive and accessible, while most of the premed classes/profs are out of touch and don't care to bridge the knowledge gap. I am grateful for the opportunity to engage in Anth research with renowned professors and have found a great, intelligent, and uplifting community!!

I love the small community and the people from many different cultures. I like my dorm right across from the center of campus and its proximity to the football fields, where I train every day. I like the food options available and the student events on the weekends.

Dartmouth is simply the most wonderful undergraduate experience I could imagine.
I’m from a large city and attended a public high school where people rarely went off to schools like Dartmouth. When I first arrived on campus, I was nervous. Since then, I’ve found an incredibly beautiful and caring community made up of remarkable individuals. Dartmouth has everything you’d expect it to: incredible faculty, small, (sometimes extremely) rigorous classes, excellent facilities, and an endless number of academic and career opportunities. It also has a many things you wouldn’t expect: its own skiway, outdoor lodges, countless traditions, and a very active social life, to name a few. The result is an experience where you live and learn in an incredibly enriching community, all in the pristine backdrop of the Upper Valley. In every sense, it lives up to its ascribed ideal of the “College on the Hill”.

Amazing academic and social college experience. There is always work to be done to improve any institution, but it is very possible to find academic support, funding for opportunities you are passionate about, and great friends here.

ai_summary: DePaul University stands out for its diverse student body and vibrant urban campus experience . Many students appreciate the flexibility of class schedules and the enthusiasm of professors, who are generally described as knowledgeable and supportive. The school provides ample opportunities for academic and professional growth, especially thanks to its location in Chicago, which allows for easy access to internships and networking. However, some students express concerns about the high tuition costs and the challenges of forming a tight-knit community , largely due to the large number of commuter students. Safety on campus, particularly near the Loop location, is also a concern among students. Overall, DePaul offers a rich educational experience shaped by its unique urban setting and a myriad of resources for student success, although some aspects—like housing quality and student life cohesion—could be improved.

reviews.text: My experience at DePaul has been ROUGH. Academically it’s okay. There are some good professors and classes and there’s some really awful ones, but most have been fine. My experience living on campus has been bad. The dorms are too expensive. The food was pretty bad, but they recently changed providers so now it is even worse. The school is massively overpriced and filled with clique-y privileged kids. There’s been some budgeting issues and they recently fired a ton of staff and are closing the loop library. School leadership is super suspicious in my opinion, and I do not trust them. There was a massive mishandling of an encampment last year (look into that before you apply here! Super bad) and there’s also been problems with them promoting AI, such as for professors to create classes and grade using AI (again, super bad!). I would not go here. Save your money and go to whatever is cheaper, because the schools are all corrupt anyways.

My experience with DePaul is that I wish they had better transportation stystems. Such as Metra. It would be so much better to have that included in their tuition instead of having to pay seperately. I like that how the area of the campus is very vibe-y and welcoming for anyone. I do feel comfortable being there because of the scenery and the people around me. It feels inclusive and diverse. I enjoy being there as a student.

What I loved was being in the heart of Chicago. My classroom was the city itself—internships in the Loop, networking events a train ride away, and a vibrant social life in Lincoln Park. The professors were consistently passionate, bringing real-world experience directly from their fields into the classroom. The quarter system was fast and kept me engaged, and there was a genuine focus on practical, career-ready skills over pure theory. The student body is diverse and down-to-earth, which created a collaborative, not cutthroat, atmosphere.

My overall experience at DePaul University has been extremely positive. DePaul offers a welcoming, student-centered environment where faculty and staff truly care about student success. Whether in the classroom or through campus resources, I’ve consistently felt supported, challenged, and encouraged to grow.

As a transfer student from College Of DuPage, stepping into DePaul has been so motivating. I have created better friendships and connections especially related to my major which is Finance. The environment at the College of Business is professional and there many opportunities related to students careers. Being in the city alone has allowed further exploration of my identity and I've been introduced to new study spots, restaurants and people. As a first generation Mexican American college student, I am very grateful to be able to further my education.

DePaul university is a great school with amazing staff that is always helpful. Being in Chicago Lincoln park makes it a great area to live in. Classes are smaller so it is easier to get more personal help from professors. Even if your professors don't have an available time for you there are always tutoring opportunities student and professor led. Having to campuses is great to your able to go downtown central Chicago and get a different feel for the city

Enrolled at DP several years ago as a part time continuing education student. Left after a couple of semesters because they insisted I take religion and other liberal arts classes before they would allow me to enroll in higher level accounting classes to help me in my career. I left and found another university to support my needs. Returned to DP years later to take a final capstone course for my MBA from another university. Professor was arrogant, kept raving about DP ranking. Students in my class were lazy, couldn’t speak fluent English and were not intelligent despite MBA status. I led our case study groups, carried the lazy ones over the finish line, got an A in the class, and was thankful my MBA had another university name on it. If you’re looking for a liberal university with plenty of foreign students, an arrogant attitude, along with a ridiculous price tag, in an area that is dangerous after dark, DP is your school.

I love DePaul! This school was my ticket out of rural Missouri. The scholarships provided are amazing if you put in the work to get them. The campus is beautiful, the classes are great, and it really deserves its spot as a top rank film school.

As an international freshman at DePaul University I would rate my overall experience as a 4.5 over 5. The biggest benefit of the university is its location: Loop as a business center and Lincoln park one of the safest neighborhoods in Chicago. For the Cybersecurity major, I would rate professors as 5 stars, but I heard bad feedback about some, so I put 4 (use ratemyprofessor website before taking classes). The academics are pretty solid and if you have some hobbies besides your major field you can take classes in numerous fields offered at DePaul either as an open elective or closing your Liberal Studies. About the campus life: housing is pretty good, but there are some dorms such as Seton and Corcoran that are really close to the CTA and the noise is really annoying. But the rooms are in pretty good condition. The food is not that good. About the party scene, there are a lot, just follow instagram profiles with parties, but my favorite are basketball matches at the weekends.

Being a Freshman at DePaul University, makes me feel excited about college student life. I though college is so hard, with all examinations and assignments, but when it have reached reality in my life, I was surprised that good studying from the very beginning is the right path to success. Also, I started to plan everything, balancing studying and my free time. Meeting a lot of new people and exploring around is also great. After taking IB DP in my high school, DePaul feels like a little relief. Overall, I'm truly grateful for myself and my family, and those who supported me in choosing DePaul University, for my undergraduate program, since I really enjoy studying here.
Overall a good experience, but I wish that my academic advisor would have been more helpful and available.

DePaul is almost perfect. It's embedded in the city of Chicago, which allows so many opportunities to break into your professional goals, and also so many options to go out. On the other hand, the party scene is not great.

My experience at DePaul University has been very positive overall. As a first-generation college student majoring in Finance with a Real Estate minor, I’ve found the university to be supportive and full of opportunities for growth. The professors are approachable and genuinely care about helping students succeed. I’ve also enjoyed the diverse campus community that reflects the city of Chicago, which has helped me connect classroom lessons to real-world experiences. DePaul’s study abroad programs have been life-changing, allowing me to travel, explore new cultures, and apply what I’ve learned academically. One area that could improve is financial support, as tuition and housing costs can be challenging for students from working-class families. Overall, DePaul has pushed me to become more independent, career-driven, and confident in my future.

So far, my overall experience at DePaul University as a sophomore student has been great. There is a lot of help provides inside and outside of school, the help that is provided inside of school has a great impact on my academic outcome since there is constant tutor groups and office hours from professors that is being provided for each student individually. It is strongly encouraged to meet with advisors since they can give you guidance, in my personal experience its been very helpful. I would say I meet with my advisor at least one time each quarter and they have always been very kind and helpful. One thing that I would like to see change would be the diversity, there is many people from different ethnicity and backgrounds but it is very limited. I am starting to see a bit of change compared to my first year at DePaul, there has been more programs made in which are open to different ethnicity's and religious beliefs.

While I am fairly new to DePaul and the two campuses, adjusting to the campus and getting to know the people has been a bit of a difficult task. The clubs provided are either Greek life or based on a strict major, there aren't many niche or interest based ones so it can be a bit difficult meeting new people. Another major thing, as a resident in the Loop Campus, there aren't many events here. The Loop Campus seems to be left in the dark when it comes to school events and activities, it can be difficult commuting to the Lincoln Park campus specifically for events so seeing more life over here would be awesome!

ai_summary: DeSales University offers a welcoming, close-knit environment where students often feel a strong sense of community and support from faculty and peers. The small class sizes foster personal relationships with professors, who are generally seen as dedicated and approachable, making it easier for students to seek help and guidance. The academic programs are rigorous, particularly in fields like nursing and business, with many students mentioning the challenging yet rewarding coursework . Student life includes a variety of activities, though some reviewers note a lack of weekend events and a relatively quiet campus atmosphere. While the campus is described as beautiful and safe, opinions about food and tuition costs vary. There's recognition of a desire for increased diversity and a more expansive campus experience, particularly regarding activities and cultural inclusivity, which some feel could enhance the college experience further. Overall, DeSales seems well-suited for students seeking a supportive, intimate educational setting, though it may not appeal to those desiring a bustling campus life.

reviews.text: Desales provides a more personal college experience allowing for success. Smaller campus, so not much to do around campus.
I want to be a DeSales student God willing in the fall 2026 for my degree in Education - Early Childhood Elementary and Special Education Dual Certification.

DeSales University is a smaller campus and everyone seems to know each other, which I like. I also enjoy its peaceful and quiet atmosphere. Playing field hockey keeps disciplined and dedicated towards my studies as well.

Overall, I had a good experience at DeSales University. The students were delightful; the instructors were strict but very willing to help. There was a strong academic sense at the school. The school clubs and activities felt very wholesome. I especially appreciated the Career Center for all the help they gave me.
Overall I have nice experience at the university. The professors are experienced and understanding.The advisors are also helpful.

Went one year before switching schools.I was too young and naive to report it when it was happening but looking back now as a true adult, I realize my freshman year English professor was a pervert creep activist and had no professional business being a professor at that school. It’s a very overpriced and underwhelming college experience.

I think that desales is an extremely clean and proper university. There are so many resources to help students, such as therapy councilors, nurses for dorming students, and so many tutors, so you're never really alone in the university. of course not every professor is as good as others but every professor does help.

DeSales University offers a welcoming, inspiring environment full of students that are more than happy to learn and participate in activities outside of academics. The campus' livelihood and the overall friendly, upbeat attitude allows any prospective, newly incoming, and returning student feel right at home. All of the events that are scattered across DeSales University help promote inclusivity, encourage engagement, and provide insight into resources and useful information that can be used for future educational or occupational endeavors. A personal favorite event DeSales University holds is first year orientation, which assists in breaking the ice between the newest additions to the community and the returning students willing to guide the freshman through the challenges that the beginning of college life brings. Something that would be nice to see is similar events like first year orientation happening throughout the whole academic year rather than just at the beginning.

I liked how the Veteran and adult student services were so accommodating compared to my first time in college. The staff has been absolutely amazing and the school is very inclusive. They are an amazing school for veterans and have made the whole transition process seamless. There is always room for improvements but so far I have not found any!
DeSales was the perfect choice for a returning student with a full-time job! I was made to feel welcome and my professors worked with me to complete my course work successfully.

I visited the university and had my brother in law tell me about it. Desales university is extremely safe and makes sure you are cared for as well as learning. The facility is wonderful and it’s filled with life from the students. There are so many wonderful areas on the campus and it’s definitely worth any high school attendees looking at. They have many food options and ample availability for all types of classes. Their programs are diverse and all enjoyable along with something going on practically every day, it gives you every oppertunity to really enjoy your experience.

I will be entering DeSales University this fall, and I have had the opportunity to participate in overnights, during which I experienced all the wonderful things the University has to offer. From the amazing food to the smaller class sizes with spectacular professors and staff. As well as the supportive coaching staff.

My experience as an adult part-time student at DeSales University has been a positive one so far. Balancing work, family, and school isn’t always easy, but the supportive professors and flexible course options have made it manageable. I’ve enjoyed the opportunity to challenge myself, learn new things, and connect with classmates who share similar goals. It’s been a rewarding journey, and I look forward to what’s ahead.

My biggest complaint with DeSales University was I felt there needed to be some reform to their requirements of the BS in Biology program. Some classes that were optional electives I felt should have been requirements for the degree, and I think it would have benefited me more in my future to have taken those courses. I did appreciate how small the campus was and that you could form personal relationships with your professors and most of your classmates.

My overall experience at DeSales University has been short, but sweet. I'm only a month into the school year, but I already feel at home. I love the small class sizes and all my professors. It's nice knowing that there's so many resources available if I need tutoring, or help finding a job. There's also plenty of clubs and sports to join, which has helped me find friends and feel apart of the community.

ai_summary: At Dominican University New York, students appreciate the close-knit community and small class sizes that foster strong relationships with professors, who are often described as caring and dedicated to student success. Many reviews highlighted that faculty genuinely invest time to help students, providing both academic and personal support. While the academic programs, particularly in nursing, are praised for their quality, there are calls for more diverse and expanded program offerings. Student life appears to be a mixed bag, with some noting a lack of excitement during weekends and limited social activities , particularly for those not involved in sports. The campus is described as beautiful and well-maintained, though there are concerns about the dormitory conditions and the overall cost of attendance, which many find steep. Overall, Dominican offers a supportive environment but could improve in areas like student support services, job placement assistance, and variety in majors.

reviews.text: I had an amazing time at Dominican University. I was estatic when I got my admissions offer. The merit scholarships help out immensely if you are a good student.
Dominican University is a beautiful college with an amazing campus with many nice people. it was easy for me to socialize which helped me because I'm a very shy person.
It was horrible. I think if the only thing good about the school is the dining hall, thats an issue.

Dominican University is a perfect school for students looking for a tight-knit community while also enjoying the college experience. Many staff members create personal relationships with the students, allowing for a support system that unlocks our full potential. I would however like to see a change in the OT program being open to struggling students beyond academic probation.
Dominican university is a passable institution for those within the area who have no desire to commute far.

While Dominican College provided a solid academic foundation, I found that the commuter nature of the school made it challenging to build a strong sense of community or social engagement outside the classroom. I’m looking for a college experience that offers more opportunities for campus involvement and peer interaction.
